true) return false;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn true;\n\t\t});\n\t\treturn id;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Update hitbox position and size\n\t* \n\t* @param id - Entity ID\n\t* @param x - Top-left X coordinate\n\t* @param y - Top-left Y coordinate\n\t* @param width - Optional width\n\t* @param height - Optional height\n\t* @returns True if hitbox was updated successfully\n\t*/\n\tupdateHitbox(id, x, y, width, height) {\n\t\tconst entity = this.physic.getEntityByUUID(id);\n\t\tif (!entity) return false;\n\t\tif (typeof width === \"number\" && typeof height === \"number\") {\n\t\t\tentity.width = Math.max(width, 1);\n\t\t\tentity.height = Math.max(height, 1);\n\t\t}\n\t\tconst entityWidth = entity.width || entity.radius * 2 || 32;\n\t\tconst entityHeight = entity.height || entity.radius * 2 || 32;\n\t\tconst centerX = x + entityWidth / 2;\n\t\tconst centerY = y + entityHeight / 2;\n\t\tentity.position.set(centerX, centerY);\n\t\treturn true;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Remove a hitbox from the physics world\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\tremoveHitbox(id, owner, kind) {\n\t\tconst entity = this.physic.getEntityByUUID(id);\n\t\tif (!entity) return false;\n\t\tthis.unbindCharacterSignalSync(entity);\n\t\tconst resolvedOwner = owner ?? entity.owner;\n\t\tif (resolvedOwner) this.emitPhysicsEntityRemove({\n\t\t\towner: resolvedOwner,\n\t\t\tentity,\n\t\t\tkind: kind ?? this.resolvePhysicsEntityKind(resolvedOwner, id)\n\t\t});\n\t\tthis.physic.removeEntity(entity);\n\t\treturn true;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Check if an entity is moving\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\tisEntityMoving(id) {\n\t\tconst entity = this.physic.getEntityByUUID(id);\n\t\tif (!entity) return false;\n\t\treturn entity.velocity.length() > .1;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Move a body in a direction\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\tmoveBody(player, direction) {\n\t\tconst entity = this.physic.getEntityByUUID(player.id);\n\t\tif (!entity) return false;\n\t\tconst speedValue = player.speed();\n\t\tlet vx = 0, vy = 0;\n\t\tswitch (direction) {\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Left:\n\t\t\t\tvx = -speedValue * this.speedScalar;\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Right:\n\t\t\t\tvx = speedValue * this.speedScalar;\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Up:\n\t\t\t\tvy = -speedValue * this.speedScalar;\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Down:\n\t\t\t\tvy = speedValue * this.speedScalar;\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t}\n\t\tentity.setVelocity({\n\t\t\tx: vx,\n\t\t\ty: vy\n\t\t});\n\t\tentity.wakeUp();\n\t\treturn true;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Stop movement for a player\n\t* \n\t* Completely stops all movement for a player, including:\n\t* - Clearing all active movement strategies (dash, linear moves, etc.)\n\t* - Setting velocity to zero\n\t* - Resetting intended direction\n\t* \n\t* This method is particularly useful when changing maps to ensure\n\t* the player doesn't carry over movement from the previous map.\n\t* \n\t* @param player - The player to stop\n\t* @returns True if the player was found and movement was stopped\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Stop player movement when changing maps\n\t* if (mapChanged) {\n\t*   map.stopMovement(player);\n\t* }\n\t* \n\t* // Stop movement when player dies\n\t* if (player.isDead()) {\n\t*   map.stopMovement(player);\n\t* }\n\t* ```\n\t* @protected\n\t*/\n\tstopMovement(player) {\n\t\tif (!this.physic.getEntityByUUID(player.id)) return false;\n\t\tthis.moveManager.stopMovement(player.id);\n\t\tplayer.pendingInputs = [];\n\t\treturn true;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Set character collision enabled\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\tsetCharacterCollisionEnabled(id, collides) {\n\t\tif (!this.physic.getEntityByUUID(id)) return false;\n\t\treturn true;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get collisions for an entity\n\t* \n\t* Returns all entities that are colliding with the specified entity.\n\t* Uses the entity's actual AABB bounds to detect collisions in all directions.\n\t* \n\t* @param id - Entity UUID to check collisions for\n\t* @returns Array of entity UUIDs that are colliding with the specified entity\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Get all entities colliding with player\n\t* const collisions = map.getCollisions('player1');\n\t* collisions.forEach(id => {\n\t*   console.log(`Entity ${id} is colliding with player`);\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetCollisions(id) {\n\t\tconst entity = this.physic.getEntityByUUID(id);\n\t\tif (!entity) return [];\n\t\tconst collider = createCollider(entity);\n\t\tif (!collider) return [];\n\t\tconst entityAABB = collider.getBounds();\n\t\tconst expandedAABB = entityAABB.expand(1);\n\t\tconst nearby = this.physic.queryAABB(expandedAABB);\n\t\tconst collisions = [];\n\t\tfor (const other of nearby) {\n\t\t\tif (other.uuid === id) continue;\n\t\t\tconst otherCollider = createCollider(other);\n\t\t\tif (!otherCollider) continue;\n\t\t\tconst otherAABB = otherCollider.getBounds();\n\t\t\tif (entityAABB.intersects(otherAABB)) collisions.push(other.uuid);\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn collisions;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get physics body (entity) for an id\n\t* @protected\n\t*/\n\tgetBody(id) {\n\t\treturn this.physic.getEntityByUUID(id);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get the current physics tick\n\t* @returns Current tick number\n\t*/\n\tgetTick() {\n\t\treturn this.physic.getTick();\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get body position in different modes\n\t* \n\t* @param id - Entity ID\n\t* @param mode - Position mode: \"center\" or \"top-left\"\n\t* @returns Position coordinates or undefined if entity not found\n\t*/\n\tgetBodyPosition(id, mode = \"center\") {\n\t\tconst entity = this.physic.getEntityByUUID(id);\n\t\tif (!entity) return void 0;\n\t\tconst centerX = entity.position.x;\n\t\tconst centerY = entity.position.y;\n\t\tif (mode === \"center\") return {\n\t\t\tx: centerX,\n\t\t\ty: centerY\n\t\t};\n\t\tconst width = entity.width || (entity.radius ? entity.radius * 2 : 32);\n\t\tconst height = entity.height || (entity.radius ? entity.radius * 2 : 32);\n\t\treturn {\n\t\t\tx: centerX - width / 2,\n\t\t\ty: centerY - height / 2\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Set body position\n\t* \n\t* @param id - Entity ID\n\t* @param x - X coordinate\n\t* @param y - Y coordinate\n\t* @param mode - Position mode: \"center\" or \"top-left\"\n\t* @returns True if position was set successfully\n\t*/\n\tsetBodyPosition(id, x, y, mode = \"center\") {\n\t\tconst entity = this.physic.getEntityByUUID(id);\n\t\tif (!entity) return;\n\t\tconst width = entity.width || (entity.radius ? entity.radius * 2 : 32);\n\t\tconst height = entity.height || (entity.radius ? entity.radius * 2 : 32);\n\t\tlet centerX = x;\n\t\tlet centerY = y;\n\t\tif (mode === \"top-left\") {\n\t\t\tcenterX = x + width / 2;\n\t\t\tcenterY = y + height / 2;\n\t\t}\n\t\tentity.position.set(centerX, centerY);\n\t\tentity.notifyPositionChange();\n\t\treturn entity;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Handle collision enter\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\t/**\n\t* Add a zone\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\taddZone(id, options) {\n\t\tconst zoneManager = this.physic.getZoneManager();\n\t\tif (this.physic.getEntityByUUID(id)) throw new Error(`Zone with id ${id} already exists as entity`);\n\t\tconst radius = options.radius;\n\t\tif (typeof radius !== \"number\" || radius <= 0) throw new Error(\"Zone radius must be a positive number\");\n\t\tlet attachedEntity;\n\t\tif (options.linkedTo) {\n\t\t\tattachedEntity = this.physic.getEntityByUUID(options.linkedTo);\n\t\t\tif (!attachedEntity) throw new Error(`Cannot link zone to unknown entity ${options.linkedTo}`);\n\t\t}\n\t\tconst \"down\",\n\t\t\tlimitedByWalls: options.limitedByWalls ?? false\n\t\t}, callbacks);\n\t\tthis._zoneIdMap = this._zoneIdMap || /* @__PURE__ */ new Map();\n\t\tthis._zoneIdMap.set(id, zoneId);\n\t\treturn id;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Remove a zone\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\tremoveZone(id) {\n\t\tconst zoneIdMap = this._zoneIdMap;\n\t\tif (!zoneIdMap) return false;\n\t\tconst zoneId = zoneIdMap.get(id);\n\t\tif (!zoneId) return false;\n\t\tthis.physic.getZoneManager().removeZone(zoneId);\n\t\tzoneIdMap.delete(id);\n\t\treturn true;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get a zone\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\tgetZone(id) {\n\t\tconst zoneIdMap = this._zoneIdMap;\n\t\tif (!zoneIdMap) return void 0;\n\t\tconst zoneId = zoneIdMap.get(id);\n\t\tif (!zoneId) return void 0;\n\t\treturn this.physic.getZoneManager().getZone(zoneId);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Register zone events\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\tregisterZoneEvents(id, onEnter, onExit) {\n\t\tconst zoneIdMap = this._zoneIdMap;\n\t\tif (!zoneIdMap) return false;\n\t\tconst zoneId = zoneIdMap.get(id);\n\t\tif (!zoneId) return false;\n\t\tconst zoneManager = this.physic.getZoneManager();\n\t\tconst callbacks = {};\n\t\tif (onEnter) e.uuid));\n\t\t};\n\t\treturn zoneManager.registerCallbacks(zoneId, callbacks);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Run post-tick updates (update zones)\n\t* @private\n\t*/\n\trunPostTickUpdates() {\n\t\tthis.physic.getZoneManager().update();\n\t}\n};\n//#endregion\n//#region ../common/src/rooms/WorldMaps.ts\n/**\n* World Maps Manager\n* \n* Manages a collection of interconnected maps and their spatial relationships\n*/\nvar WorldMapsManager = class {\n\tconstructor() {\n\t\tthis.maps = /* @__PURE__ */ new Map();\n\t\tthis.spatialIndex = /* @__PURE__ */ new Map();\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Configure the world maps\n\t* \n\t* @param configs - Array of map configurations\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const worldMaps = new WorldMapsManager();\n\t* worldMaps.configure([\n\t*   { id: \"town\", worldX: 0, worldY: 0, width: 1024, height: 768 },\n\t*   { id: \"forest\", worldX: 1024, worldY: 0, width: 1024, height: 768 }\n\t* ]);\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tconfigure(configs) {\n\t\tthis.maps.clear();\n\t\tthis.spatialIndex.clear();\n\t\tfor (const config of configs) {\n\t\t\tconst worldMap = {\n\t\t\t\tid: config.id,\n\t\t\t\tx: config.worldX,\n\t\t\t\ty: config.worldY,\n\t\t\t\twidth: config.width,\n\t\t\t\theight: config.height,\n\t\t\t\tworldX: config.worldX,\n\t\t\t\tworldY: config.worldY,\n\t\t\t\twidthPx: config.width,\n\t\t\t\theightPx: config.height,\n\t\t\t\ttileWidth: config.tileWidth ?? 32,\n\t\t\t\ttileHeight: config.tileHeight ?? 32\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tthis.maps.set(config.id, worldMap);\n\t\t\tthis.spatialIndex.set(`${config.worldX},${config.worldY}`, config.id);\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Remove a map from the world by its id\n\t* \n\t* Deletes the map from the internal registry and spatial index.\n\t* \n\t* @param mapId - Map identifier\n\t* @returns True if a map was removed, false otherwise\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const removed = world.removeMap(\"forest\");\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tremoveMap(mapId) {\n\t\tconst map = this.maps.get(mapId);\n\t\tif (!map) return false;\n\t\tthis.maps.delete(mapId);\n\t\tthis.spatialIndex.delete(`${map.worldX},${map.worldY}`);\n\t\treturn true;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Find adjacent maps based on various search strategies\n\t* \n\t* Supports three search modes:\n\t* - PositionBox: collect maps intersecting the given box\n\t* - Direction: collect maps adjacent in the given direction\n\t* - Point: collect the map containing the given world point\n\t* \n\t* The given `map` can be any object exposing `worldX`, `worldY`, `width`, `height` properties\n\t* (e.g. your `RpgMap` instance or a `WorldMapInfo`).\n\t* \n\t* @param map - The source map\n\t* @param search - Search strategy (box, direction or point)\n\t* @returns Array of matching adjacent map infos\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Point\n\t* world.getAdjacentMaps(currentMap, { x: 1024, y: 0 });\n\t* \n\t* // Direction\n\t* world.getAdjacentMaps(currentMap, Direction.Up);\n\t* \n\t* // Box\n\t* world.getAdjacentMaps(currentMap, { minX: 0, minY: 0, maxX: 2048, maxY: 1024 });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetAdjacentMaps(map, search) {\n\t\tconst maps = Array.from(this.maps.values());\n\t\tif (typeof search === \"number\") {\n\t\t\tconst src = map;\n\t\t\treturn maps.filter((m) => {\n\t\t\t\tconst horizontallyOverlapsOrTouches = Math.max(src.worldX, m.worldX) <= Math.min(src.worldX + src.widthPx, m.worldX + m.widthPx);\n\t\t\t\tconst verticallyOverlapsOrTouches = Math.max(src.worldY, m.worldY) <= Math.min(src.worldY + src.heightPx, m.worldY + m.heightPx);\n\t\t\t\tconst marginLeftRight = src.tileWidth / 2;\n\t\t\t\tconst marginTopDown = src.tileHeight / 2;\n\t\t\t\tswitch (search) {\n\t\t\t\t\tcase 0: return verticallyOverlapsOrTouches && m.worldY + m.heightPx - marginTopDown === src.worldY;\n\t\t\t\t\tcase 1: return verticallyOverlapsOrTouches && m.worldY + marginTopDown === src.worldY + src.heightPx;\n\t\t\t\t\tcase 2: return horizontallyOverlapsOrTouches && m.worldX + m.widthPx - marginLeftRight === src.worldX;\n\t\t\t\t\tcase 3: return horizontallyOverlapsOrTouches && m.worldX + marginLeftRight === src.worldX + src.widthPx;\n\t\t\t\t\tdefault: return false;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (\"x\" in search && \"y\" in search) {\n\t\t\tconst found = maps.find((m) => search.x >= m.worldX && search.x < m.worldX + m.widthPx && search.y >= m.worldY && search.y < m.worldY + m.heightPx);\n\t\t\treturn found ? [found] : [];\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (\"minX\" in search) {\n\t\t\tconst { minX, minY, maxX, maxY } = search;\n\t\t\treturn maps.filter((m) => {\n\t\t\t\tconst aLeft = m.worldX;\n\t\t\t\tconst aRight = m.worldX + m.widthPx;\n\t\t\t\tconst aTop = m.worldY;\n\t\t\t\tconst aBottom = m.worldY + m.heightPx;\n\t\t\t\treturn aLeft < maxX && aRight > minX && aTop < maxY && aBottom > minY;\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn [];\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get map information by ID\n\t* \n\t* @param mapId - The unique identifier of the module hook\n\t* @param args - Arguments to pass to the hook functions\n\t* @returns An Observable that emits an array with all hook results when subscribed\n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Call all hook functions for the ID 'my-namespace-type-hook'\n\t* callHooks('my-namespace-type-hook', { x: 10, y: 20 }).subscribe(results => {\n\t*   console.log('Hook results:', results);  // Array of all hook function results\n\t* });\n\t* \n\t* // Hook functions are only executed when you subscribe to the returned Observable\n\t* const observable = callHooks('my-namespace-type-hook', player);\n\t* // ... later ...\n\t* observable.subscribe(results => {\n\t*   // Process results array\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tcallHooks(hookId, ...args) {\n\t\tif (!this.moduleHooks[hookId]) this.moduleHooks[hookId] = new Subject();\n\t\treturn new Observable((subscriber) => {\n\t\t\tif (this.hookFunctions[hookId] && this.hookFunctions[hookId].length > 0) {\n\t\t\t\tconst subscription = from(this.hookFunctions[hookId]).pipe(mergeMap((hookFunction) => {\n\t\t\t\t\tlet result;\n\t\t\t\t\ttry {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tresult = hookFunction(...args);\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn from(Promise.resolve(result));\n\t\t\t\t\t} catch (error) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tsubscriber.error(error);\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn [];\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}), toArray()).subscribe({\n\t\t\t\t\tnext: (results) => {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.moduleHooks[hookId].next(results);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tsubscriber.next(results);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tsubscriber.complete();\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\terror: (err) => {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tsubscriber.error(err);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t\treturn () => {\n\t\t\t\t\tsubscription.unsubscribe();\n\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tsubscriber.next([]);\n\t\t\t\tsubscriber.complete();\n\t\t\t\treturn () => {};\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t});\n\t}\n};\n/**\n* Provide modules configuration to Angular Dependency Injection\n* Registers hook functions for later execution\n*\n* @param modules - min + 1) + min);\n}\nfunction isFunction(val) {\n\treturn {}.toString.call(val) === \"[object Function]\";\n}\nfunction isArray(val) {\n\treturn isInstanceOf(val, Array);\n}\nfunction isString(val) {\n\treturn typeof val == \"string\";\n}\nfunction isInstanceOf(val, _class) {\n\treturn val instanceof _class;\n}\nfunction arrayUniq(array) {\n\treturn [...new Set(array)];\n}\nfunction arrayFlat(array) {\n\treturn array.reduce((acc, val) => acc.concat(val), []);\n}\nfunction capitalize(s) {\n\tif (typeof s !== \"string\") return \"\";\n\treturn s.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + s.slice(1);\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region ../common/src/PrebuiltGui.ts\n/** \n* \n* Pre-made GUIs already exist. // Returns value between -1 and 1\n* \n* // With seed for deterministic results\n* const seededNoise = new PerlinNoise2D(12345);\n* ```\n* \n* @example\n* ```ts\n* // Use in movement system for smooth random directions\n* const noise = new PerlinNoise2D();\n* const time = Date.now() * 0.001;\n* const direction = Math.floor(noise.get(player.x(), player.y(), time) * 4) % 4;\n* ```\n*/\nvar PerlinNoise2D = class {\n\t/**\n\t* Creates a new Perlin noise generator\n\t* \n\t* @param seed - Optional seed for deterministic noise generation. If not provided, uses a default seed.\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const noise = new PerlinNoise2D(12345);\n\t* const value = noise.get(10, 20);\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tconstructor(seed = 0) {\n\t\tthis.permutation = this.generatePermutation(seed);\n\t\tthis.p = [...this.permutation, ...this.permutation];\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Generates a permutation table based on seed\n\t* \n\t* @param seed - Seed value for permutation generation\n\t* @returns Array of 256 shuffled values\n\t*/\n\tgeneratePermutation(seed) {\n\t\tconst p = [];\n\t\tfor (let i = 0; i < 256; i++) p[i] = i;\n\t\tlet state = seed;\n\t\tconst lcg = () => {\n\t\t\tstate = state * 1103515245 + 12345 & 2147483647;\n\t\t\treturn state;\n\t\t};\n\t\tfor (let i = 255; i > 0; i--) {\n\t\t\tconst j = lcg() % (i + 1);\n\t\t\t[p[i], p[j]] = [p[j], p[i]];\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn p;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Fade function for smooth interpolation (ease curve)\n\t* \n\t* @param t - Value between 0 and 1\n\t* @returns Smoothed value between 0 and 1\n\t*/\n\tfade(t) {\n\t\treturn t * t * t * (t * (t * 6 - 15) + 10);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Linear interpolation\n\t* \n\t* @param a - Start value\n\t* @param b - End value\n\t* @param t - Interpolation factor (0 to 1)\n\t* @returns Interpolated value\n\t*/\n\tlerp(a, b, t) {\n\t\treturn a + t * (b - a);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Gradient function - generates a pseudo-random gradient vector\n\t* \n\t* @param hash - Hash value from permutation table\n\t* @param x - X component\n\t* @param y - Y component\n\t* @returns Dot product of gradient and position\n\t*/\n\tgrad(hash, x, y) {\n\t\tswitch (hash & 3) {\n\t\t\tcase 0: return x + y;\n\t\t\tcase 1: return -x + y;\n\t\t\tcase 2: return x - y;\n\t\t\tcase 3: return -x - y;\n\t\t\tdefault: return 0;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Gets the noise value at the specified 2D coordinates\n\t* \n\t* Returns a value between approximately -1 and 1, though values near the edges\n\t* are less common. For practical use, you may want to clamp or normalize the result.\n\t* \n\t* @param x - X coordinate\n\t* @param y - Y coordinate\n\t* @param scale - Optional scale factor (default: 0.1). Lower values create smoother, larger patterns.\n\t* @returns Noise value between approximately -1 and 1\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const noise = new PerlinNoise2D();\n\t* const value = noise.get(10, 20); // Basic usage\n\t* const scaled = noise.get(10, 20, 0.05); // Smoother pattern\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tget(x, y, scale = .1) {\n\t\tx *= scale;\n\t\ty *= scale;\n\t\tconst X = Math.floor(x) & 255;\n\t\tconst Y = Math.floor(y) & 255;\n\t\tx -= Math.floor(x);\n\t\ty -= Math.floor(y);\n\t\tconst u = this.fade(x);\n\t\tconst v = this.fade(y);\n\t\tconst A = this.p[X] + Y;\n\t\tconst AA = this.p[A];\n\t\tconst AB = this.p[A + 1];\n\t\tconst B = this.p[X + 1] + Y;\n\t\tconst BA = this.p[B];\n\t\tconst BB = this.p[B + 1];\n\t\treturn this.lerp(this.lerp(this.grad(this.p[AA], x, y), this.grad(this.p[BA], x - 1, y), u), this.lerp(this.grad(this.p[AB], x, y - 1), this.grad(this.p[BB], x - 1, y - 1), u), v);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Gets a normalized noise value between 0 and 1\n\t* \n\t* Convenience method that normalizes the noise output to a 0-1 range.\n\t* \n\t* @param x - X coordinate\n\t* @param y - Y coordinate\n\t* @param scale - Optional scale factor (default: 0.1)\n\t* @returns Noise value between 0 and 1\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const noise = new PerlinNoise2D();\n\t* const normalized = noise.getNormalized(10, 20);\n\t* // Returns value between 0 and 1\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetNormalized(x, y, scale = .1) {\n\t\treturn (this.get(x, y, scale) + 1) * .5;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Gets a noise value mapped to a specific range\n\t* \n\t* Maps the noise output to a custom min-max range.\n\t* \n\t* @param x - X coordinate\n\t* @param y - Y coordinate\n\t* @param min - Minimum output value\n\t* @param max - Maximum output value\n\t* @param scale - Optional scale factor (default: 0.1)\n\t* @returns Noise value between min and max\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const noise = new PerlinNoise2D();\n\t* const direction = noise.getRange(10, 20, 0, 3); // Returns 0, 1, 2, or 3\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetRange(x, y, min, max, scale = .1) {\n\t\treturn min + this.getNormalized(x, y, scale) * (max - min);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Gets an integer noise value in a specific range (inclusive)\n\t* \n\t* Useful for selecting discrete values like array indices or enum values.\n\t* \n\t* @param x - X coordinate\n\t* @param y - Y coordinate\n\t* @param min - Minimum integer value (inclusive)\n\t* @param max - Maximum integer value (inclusive)\n\t* @param scale - Optional scale factor (default: 0.1)\n\t* @returns Integer noise value between min and max (inclusive)\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const noise = new PerlinNoise2D();\n\t* const directionIndex = noise.getInt(10, 20, 0, 3); // Returns 0, 1, 2, or 3\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetInt(x, y, min, max, scale = .1) {\n\t\tconst value = this.getRange(x, y, min, max + 1, scale);\n\t\treturn Math.floor(value);\n\t}\n};\n//#endregion\n//#region ../common/src/Presets.ts\nvar MAXHP = \"maxHp\";\nvar MAXSP = \"maxSp\";\nvar ATK = \"atk\";\nvar PDEF = \"pdef\";\nvar SDEF = \"sdef\";\nvar STR = \"str\";\nvar AGI = \"agi\";\nvar INT = \"int\";\nvar DEX = \"dex\";\n//#endregion\n//#region ../../node_modules/.pnpm/@signe+di@2.9.0/node_modules/@signe/di/dist/index.js\nvar __defProp = Object.defineProperty;\nvar __name = (target, value) => __defProp(target, \"name\", {\n\tvalue,\n\tconfigurable: true\n});\nvar DEFAULT_INSTANCE_KEY = \"__default__\";\nfunction toTokenName(token) {\n\treturn typeof token === \"function\" ? token.name : token;\n}\n__name(toTokenName, \"toTokenName\");\nfunction toInstanceKey(name) {\n\treturn name ?? Inputs keep setting the base velocity, and this strategy adds a decaying\n* impulse each physics step for a short duration.\n*\n* ## Design\n*\n* - The built-in physics `Knockback` strategy overwrites velocity every frame.\n*   When inputs also change velocity, the two systems compete and can cause visible jitter.\n* - This strategy avoids the \"tug of war\" by reading the current velocity and adding\n*   the knockback impulse on top.\n* - When finished, it does **not** force velocity to zero, so player input remains responsive.\n*\n* @example\n* ```ts\n* // Add a short impulse to the right, while player can still steer\n* await player.addMovement(new AdditiveKnockback({ x: 1, y: 0 }, 5, 0.3));\n* ```\n*/\nvar AdditiveKnockback = class {\n\tconstructor(direction, initialSpeed, duration, decayFactor = .35) {\n\t\tthis.duration = duration;\n\t\tthis.decayFactor = decayFactor;\n\t\tthis.elapsed = 0;\n\t\tconst magnitude = Math.hypot(direction.x, direction.y);\n\t\tthis.direction = magnitude > 0 ? {\n\t\t\tx: direction.x / magnitude,\n\t\t\ty: direction.y / magnitude\n\t\t} : {\n\t\t\tx: 1,\n\t\t\ty: 0\n\t\t};\n\t\tthis.currentSpeed = initialSpeed;\n\t}\n\tupdate(body, dt) {\n\t\tthis.elapsed += dt;\n\t\tif (this.elapsed > this.duration) return;\n\t\tconst impulseX = this.direction.x * this.currentSpeed;\n\t\tconst impulseY = this.direction.y * this.currentSpeed;\n\t\tbody.setVelocity({\n\t\t\tx: body.velocity.x + impulseX,\n\t\t\ty: body.velocity.y + impulseY\n\t\t});\n\t\tconst decay = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, this.decayFactor));\n\t\tif (decay === 0) this.currentSpeed = 0;\n\t\telse if (decay !== 1) this.currentSpeed *= Math.pow(decay, dt);\n\t}\n\tisFinished() {\n\t\treturn this.elapsed >= this.duration;\n\t}\n};\nfunction wait(sec) {\n\treturn new Promise((resolve) => {\n\t\tsetTimeout(resolve, sec * 1e3);\n\t});\n}\nvar Frequency = /* @__PURE__ */ function(Frequency) {\n\tFrequency[Frequency[\"Lowest\"] = 600] = \"Lowest\";\n\tFrequency[Frequency[\"Lower\"] = 400] = \"Lower\";\n\tFrequency[Frequency[\"Low\"] = 200] = \"Low\";\n\tFrequency[Frequency[\"High\"] = 100] = \"High\";\n\tFrequency[Frequency[\"Higher\"] = 50] = \"Higher\";\n\tFrequency[Frequency[\"Highest\"] = 25] = \"Highest\";\n\tFrequency[Frequency[\"None\"] = 0] = \"None\";\n\treturn Frequency;\n}({});\nvar Speed = /* @__PURE__ */ function(Speed) {\n\tSpeed[Speed[\"Slowest\"] = .2] = \"Slowest\";\n\tSpeed[Speed[\"Slower\"] = .5] = \"Slower\";\n\tSpeed[Speed[\"Slow\"] = 1] = \"Slow\";\n\tSpeed[Speed[\"Normal\"] = 3] = \"Normal\";\n\tSpeed[Speed[\"Fast\"] = 5] = \"Fast\";\n\tSpeed[Speed[\"Faster\"] = 7] = \"Faster\";\n\tSpeed[Speed[\"Fastest\"] = 10] = \"Fastest\";\n\treturn Speed;\n}({});\nvar MoveList = class MoveList {\n\tstatic {\n\t\tthis.perlinNoise = new PerlinNoise2D();\n\t}\n\tstatic {\n\t\tthis.randomCounter = 0;\n\t}\n\tstatic {\n\t\tthis.callCounter = 0;\n\t}\n\tstatic {\n\t\tthis.playerMoveStates = /* @__PURE__ */ new Map();\n\t}\n\tstatic {\n\t\tthis.STUCK_THRESHOLD = 2;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Clears the movement state for a specific player\n\t* \n\t* Should be called when a player changes map or is destroyed to prevent\n\t* memory leaks and stale stuck detection data.\n\t* \n\t* @param playerId - The ID of the player to clear state for\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Clear state when player leaves map\n\t* Move.clearPlayerState(player.id);\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tstatic clearPlayerState(playerId) {\n\t\tMoveList.playerMoveStates.delete(playerId);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Clears all player movement states\n\t* \n\t* Useful for cleanup during server shutdown or when resetting game state.\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Clear all states on server shutdown\n\t* Move.clearAllPlayerStates();\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tstatic clearAllPlayerStates() {\n\t\tMoveList.playerMoveStates.clear();\n\t}\n\trepeatMove(direction, repeat) {\n\t\tif (!Number.isFinite(repeat) || repeat < 0 || repeat > 1e4) {\n\t\t\tconsole.warn(\"Invalid repeat value:\", repeat, \"using default value 1\");\n\t\t\trepeat = 1;\n\t\t}\n\t\trepeat = Math.floor(repeat);\n\t\tif (repeat < 0 || repeat > Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER || !Number.isSafeInteger(repeat)) {\n\t\t\tconst playerSpeed = typeof player.speed === \"function\" ? player.speed() : player.speed;\n\t\t\tif (!playerSpeed || playerSpeed <= 0) {\n\t\t\t\tconsole.warn(\"Invalid player speed:\", (!Number.isFinite(repeatTile) || repeatTile < 0 || repeatTile > 1e4) {\n\t\t\t\tconsole.warn(\"Calculated repeatTile is invalid:\", repeatTile, \"using original repeat:\", repeat);\n\t\t\t\treturn this[direction](repeat);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (!Number.isSafeInteger(repeatTile)) {\n\t\t\t\tconsole.warn(\"repeatTile is not a safe integer:\", repeatTile, \"using original repeat:\", repeat);\n\t\t\t\treturn this[direction](repeat);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttry {\n\t\t\t\treturn this[direction](repeatTile);\n\t\t\t} catch (error) {\n\t\t\t\tconsole.error(\"Error calling direction method with repeatTile:\", repeatTile, error);\n\t\t\t\treturn this[direction](repeat);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\tright(repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn this.repeatMove(Direction.Right, repeat);\n\t}\n\tleft(repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn this.repeatMove(Direction.Left, repeat);\n\t}\n\tup(repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn this.repeatMove(Direction.Up, repeat);\n\t}\n\tdown(repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn this.repeatMove(Direction.Down, repeat);\n\t}\n\twait(sec) {\n\t\treturn wait(sec);\n\t}\n\trandom(repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn new Array(repeat).fill(null).map(() => \"tileHeight\");\n\t}\n\ttileDown(repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn this.repeatTileMove(\"down\", repeat, \"tileHeight\");\n\t}\n\ttileRandom(repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn (player, map) => {\n\t\t\tlet directions = [];\n\t\t\tfor (let i = 0; i < repeat; i++) {\n\t\t\t\tconst randFn = [\n\t\t\t\t\tthis.tileRight(),\n\t\t\t\t\tthis.tileLeft(),\n\t\t\t\t\tthis.tileUp(),\n\t\t\t\t\tthis.tileDown()\n\t\t\t\t][random(0, 3)];\n\t\t\t\tdirections = [...directions, ...randFn(player, map)];\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn directions;\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\t_awayFromPlayerDirection(player, otherPlayer) {\n\t\tconst directionOtherPlayer = otherPlayer.getDirection();\n\t\tlet newDirection = Direction.Down;\n\t\tswitch (directionOtherPlayer) {\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Left:\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Right:\n\t\t\t\tif (otherPlayer.x() > player.x()) newDirection = Direction.Left;\n\t\t\t\telse newDirection = Direction.Right;\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Up:\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Down:\n\t\t\t\tif (otherPlayer.y() > player.y()) newDirection = Direction.Up;\n\t\t\t\telse newDirection = Direction.Down;\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn newDirection;\n\t}\n\t_towardPlayerDirection(player, otherPlayer) {\n\t\tconst directionOtherPlayer = otherPlayer.getDirection();\n\t\tlet newDirection = Direction.Down;\n\t\tswitch (directionOtherPlayer) {\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Left:\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Right:\n\t\t\t\tif (otherPlayer.x() > player.x()) newDirection = Direction.Right;\n\t\t\t\telse newDirection = Direction.Left;\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Up:\n\t\t\tcase Direction.Down:\n\t\t\t\tif (otherPlayer.y() > player.y()) newDirection = Direction.Down;\n\t\t\t\telse newDirection = Direction.Up;\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn newDirection;\n\t}\n\t_awayFromPlayer({ isTile, typeMov }, otherPlayer, repeat = 1) {\n\t\tconst method = (dir) => {\n\t\t\tconst direction = DirectionNames[dir] || \"down\";\n\t\t\treturn this[isTile ? \"tile\" + capitalize(direction) : direction](repeat);\n\t\t};\n\t\treturn (player, map) => {\n\t\t\tlet newDirection = Direction.Down;\n\t\t\tswitch (typeMov) {\n\t\t\t\tcase \"away\":\n\t\t\t\t\tnewDirection = this._awayFromPlayerDirection(player, otherPlayer);\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\tcase \"toward\":\n\t\t\t\t\tnewDirection = this._towardPlayerDirection(player, otherPlayer);\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlet direction = method(newDirection);\n\t\t\tif (isFunction$1(direction)) direction = direction(player, map);\n\t\t\treturn direction;\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\ttowardPlayer(player, repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn this._awayFromPlayer({\n\t\t\tisTile: false,\n\t\t\ttypeMov: \"toward\"\n\t\t}, player, repeat);\n\t}\n\ttileTowardPlayer(player, repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn this._awayFromPlayer({\n\t\t\tisTile: true,\n\t\t\ttypeMov: \"toward\"\n\t\t}, player, repeat);\n\t}\n\tawayFromPlayer(player, repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn this._awayFromPlayer({\n\t\t\tisTile: false,\n\t\t\ttypeMov: \"away\"\n\t\t}, player, repeat);\n\t}\n\ttileAwayFromPlayer(player, repeat = 1) {\n\t\treturn this._awayFromPlayer({\n\t\t\tisTile: true,\n\t\t\ttypeMov: \"away\"\n\t\t}, player, repeat);\n\t}\n\tturnLeft() {\n\t\treturn \"turn-\" + Direction.Left;\n\t}\n\tturnRight() {\n\t\treturn \"turn-\" + Direction.Right;\n\t}\n\tturnUp() {\n\t\treturn \"turn-\" + Direction.Up;\n\t}\n\tturnDown() {\n\t\treturn \"turn-\" + Direction.Down;\n\t}\n\tturnRandom() {\n\t\tconst directionIndex = this.getRandomDirectionIndex();\n\t\treturn [\n\t\t\tthis.turnRight(),\n\t\t\tthis.turnLeft(),\n\t\t\tthis.turnUp(),\n\t\t\tthis.turnDown()\n\t\t][directionIndex];\n\t}\n\tturnAwayFromPlayer(otherPlayer) {\n\t\treturn (player) => {\n\t\t\treturn \"turn-\" + this._awayFromPlayerDirection(player, otherPlayer);\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\tturnTowardPlayer(otherPlayer) {\n\t\treturn (player) => {\n\t\t\treturn \"turn-\" + this._towardPlayerDirection(player, otherPlayer);\n\t\t};\n\t}\n};\nvar DirectionNames = {\n\t[Direction.Up]: \"up\",\n\t[Direction.Down]: \"down\",\n\t[Direction.Left]: \"left\",\n\t[Direction.Right]: \"right\"\n};\nvar Move = new MoveList();\n/**\n* Move Manager mixin\n* \n* Adds comprehensive movement management capabilities to a player class.\n* Provides access to all available movement strategies and utility methods\n* for common movement patterns.\n* \n* ## Features\n* - **Strategy Management**: Add, remove, and query movement strategies\n* - **Predefined Movements**: Quick access to common movement patterns\n* - **Composite Movements**: Combine multiple strategies\n* - **Physics Integration**: Seamless integration with the deterministic @rpgjs/physic engine\n* \n* ## Available Movement Strategies\n* - `LinearMove`: Constant velocity movement\n* - `Dash`: Quick burst movement\n* - `Knockback`: Push effect with decay\n* - `PathFollow`: Follow waypoint sequences\n* - `Oscillate`: Back-and-forth patterns\n* - `SeekAvoid`: AI pathfinding with obstacle avoidance\n* - `LinearRepulsion`: Smoother obstacle avoidance\n* - `IceMovement`: Slippery surface physics\n* - `ProjectileMovement`: Ballistic trajectories\n* - `CompositeMovement`: Combine multiple strategies\n* \n* @param Base - The base class to extend\n* @returns A new class with comprehensive movement management capabilities\n* \n* @example\n* ```ts\n* // Basic usage\n* class MyPlayer extends WithMoveManager(RpgCommonPlayer) {\n*   onInput(direction: { x: number, y: number }) {\n*     // Apply dash movement on input\n*     this.dash(direction, 8, 200);\n*   }\n* \n*   onIceTerrain() {\n*     // Switch to ice physics\n*     this.clearMovements();\n*     this.applyIceMovement({ x: 1, y: 0 }, 4);\n*   }\n* \n*   createPatrol() {\n*     // Create patrol path\n*     const waypoints = [\n*       { x: 100, y: 100 },\n*       { x: 300, y: 100 },\n*       { x: 300, y: 300 }\n*     ];\n*     this.followPath(waypoints, 2, true);\n*   }\n* }\n* ```\n*/\n/**\n* Move Manager Mixin\n* \n* Provides comprehensive movement management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n* various types of movement including pathfinding, physics-based movement, route following,\n* and advanced movement strategies like dashing, knockback, and projectile movement.\n* \n* @param Base - The base class to extend with movement management\n* @returns Extended class with movement management methods\n* \n* @example\n* ```ts\n* class MyPlayer extends WithMoveManager(BasePlayer) {\n*   constructor() {\n*     super();\n*     this.frequency = Frequency.High;\n*   }\n* }\n* \n* const player = new MyPlayer();\n* player.moveTo({ x: 100, y: 100 });\n* player.dash({ x: 1, y: 0 }, 8, 200);\n* ```\n*/\nfunction WithMoveManager(Base) {\n\tconst baseProto = Base.prototype;\n\tclass WithMoveManagerClass extends Base {\n\t\tconstructor(..._args) {\n\t\t\tsuper(..._args);\n\t\t\tthis._infiniteRoutes = null;\n\t\t\tthis._finishRoute = null;\n\t\t\tthis._isInfiniteRouteActive = false;\n\t\t}\n\t\tsetAnimation(animationName, nbTimes) {\n\t\t\tif (typeof baseProto.setAnimation === \"function\") baseProto.setAnimation.call(this, animationName, nbTimes);\n\t\t}\n\t\tshowComponentAnimation(id, params) {\n\t\t\tif (typeof baseProto.showComponentAnimation === \"function\") baseProto.showComponentAnimation.call(this, id, params);\n\t\t}\n\t\tset throughOtherPlayer(value) {\n\t\t\tthis._throughOtherPlayer.set(value);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget throughOtherPlayer() {\n\t\t\treturn this._throughOtherPlayer();\n\t\t}\n\t\tset through(value) {\n\t\t\tthis._through.set(value);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget through() {\n\t\t\treturn this._through();\n\t\t}\n\t\tset throughEvent(value) {\n\t\t\tthis._throughEvent.set(value);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget throughEvent() {\n\t\t\treturn this._throughEvent();\n\t\t}\n\t\tset frequency(value) {\n\t\t\tthis._frequency.set(value);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget frequency() {\n\t\t\treturn this._frequency();\n\t\t}\n\t\tset directionFixed(value) {\n\t\t\tthis._directionFixed.set(value);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget directionFixed() {\n\t\t\treturn this._directionFixed();\n\t\t}\n\t\tset animationFixed(value) {\n\t\t\tthis._animationFixed.set(value);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget animationFixed() {\n\t\t\treturn this._animationFixed();\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Add a movement strategy to this entity\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Returns a Promise that resolves when the movement completes (when `isFinished()` returns true).\n\t\t* If the strategy doesn't implement `isFinished()`, the Promise resolves immediately.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param strategy - The movement strategy to add\n\t\t* @param options - Optional callbacks for start and completion events\n\t\t* @returns Promise that resolves when the movement completes\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Fire and forget\n\t\t* player.addMovement(new LinearMove({ x: 1, y: 0 }, 200));\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Wait for completion\n\t\t* await player.addMovement(new Dash(10, { x: 1, y: 0 }, 200));\n\t\t* console.log('Dash completed!');\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // With callbacks\n\t\t* await player.addMovement(new Knockback({ x: -1, y: 0 }, 5, 300), {\n\t\t*   onStart: () => console.log('Knockback started'),\n\t\t*   onComplete: () => console.log('Knockback completed')\n\t\t* });\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\taddMovement(strategy, options) {\n\t\t\tconst map = this.getCurrentMap();\n\t\t\tif (!map) return Promise.resolve();\n\t\t\tconst playerId = this.id;\n\t\t\treturn map.moveManager.add(playerId, strategy, options);\n\t\t}\n\t\tremoveMovement(strategy) {\n\t\t\tconst map = this.getCurrentMap();\n\t\t\tif (!map) return false;\n\t\t\tconst playerId = this.id;\n\t\t\treturn map.moveManager.remove(playerId, strategy);\n\t\t}\n\t\tclearMovements() {\n\t\t\tconst map = this.getCurrentMap();\n\t\t\tif (!map) return;\n\t\t\tconst playerId = this.id;\n\t\t\tmap.moveManager.clear(playerId);\n\t\t}\n\t\thasActiveMovements() {\n\t\t\tconst map = this.getCurrentMap();\n\t\t\tif (!map) return false;\n\t\t\treturn map.moveManager.hasActiveStrategies(this.id);\n\t\t}\n\t\tgetActiveMovements() {\n\t\t\tconst map = this.getCurrentMap();\n\t\t\tif (!map) return [];\n\t\t\treturn map.moveManager.getStrategies(this.id);\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Move toward a target player or position using AI pathfinding\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Uses the `SeekAvoid` strategy to navigate toward the target while avoiding obstacles.\n\t\t* The movement speed is based on the player's current `speed` and `frequency` settings,\n\t\t* scaled appropriately.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param target - Target player or position `{ x, y }` to move toward\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Move toward another player\n\t\t* player.moveTo(otherPlayer);\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Move toward a specific position\n\t\t* player.moveTo({ x: 200, y: 150 });\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tmoveTo(target) {\n\t\t\tconst map = this.getCurrentMap();\n\t\t\tif (!map) return;\n\t\t\tconst playerId = this.id;\n\t\t\tconst engine = map.physic;\n\t\t\tconst playerSpeed = this.speed();\n\t\t\tconst rawFrequency = this.frequency;\n\t\t\tconst playerFrequency = typeof rawFrequency === \"function\" ? rawFrequency() : rawFrequency;\n\t\t\tconst frequencyScale = playerFrequency > 0 ? Frequency.High / playerFrequency : 1;\n\t\t\tconst normalizedFrequencyScale = Number.isFinite(frequencyScale) && frequencyScale > 0 ? frequencyScale : 1;\n\t\t\tconst conflictingStrategies = this.getActiveMovements().filter((s) => s instanceof SeekAvoid || s instanceof Dash || s instanceof Knockback || s instanceof LinearRepulsion);\n\t\t\tif (conflictingStrategies.length > 0) conflictingStrategies.forEach((s) => this.removeMovement(s));\n\t\t\tif (\"id\" in target) {\n\t\t\t\tconst targetProvider = () => {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn map.getBody(target.id) ?? null;\n\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t\tconst maxSpeed = playerSpeed * 45 * normalizedFrequencyScale;\n\t\t\t\tmap.moveManager.add(playerId, new SeekAvoid(engine, targetProvider, maxSpeed, 140, 80, 48));\n\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tconst staticTarget = new Entity({\n\t\t\t\tposition: {\n\t\t\t\t\tx: target.x,\n\t\t\t\t\ty: target.y\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tmass: Infinity\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t\tstaticTarget.freeze();\n\t\t\tconst maxSpeed = playerSpeed * 20 * normalizedFrequencyScale;\n\t\t\tmap.moveManager.add(playerId, new SeekAvoid(engine, () => staticTarget, maxSpeed, 140, 80, 48));\n\t\t}\n\t\tstopMoveTo() {\n\t\t\tif (!this.getCurrentMap()) return;\n\t\t\tlet strategies = [];\n\t\t\ttry {\n\t\t\t\tstrategies = this.getActiveMovements();\n\t\t\t} catch (error) {\n\t\t\t\tif ((error?.message ?? \"\").includes(\"unable to resolve entity\")) return;\n\t\t\t\tthrow error;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tconst toRemove = strategies.filter((s) => s instanceof SeekAvoid || s instanceof LinearRepulsion);\n\t\t\tif (toRemove.length > 0) toRemove.forEach((strategy) => {\n\t\t\t\tthis.removeMovement(strategy);\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Perform a dash movement in the specified direction\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Creates a burst of velocity for a fixed duration. The total speed is calculated\n\t\t* by adding the player's base speed (`this.speed()`) to the additional dash speed.\n\t\t* This ensures faster players also dash faster proportionally.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* With default speed=4 and additionalSpeed=4: total = 8 (same as original default)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param direction - Normalized direction vector `{ x, y }` for the dash\n\t\t* @param additionalSpeed - Extra speed added on top of base speed (default: 4)\n\t\t* @param duration - Duration in milliseconds (default: 200)\n\t\t* @param options - Optional callbacks for movement events\n\t\t* @returns Promise that resolves when the dash completes\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Dash to the right and wait for completion\n\t\t* await player.dash({ x: 1, y: 0 });\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Powerful dash with callbacks\n\t\t* await player.dash({ x: 0, y: -1 }, 12, 300, {\n\t\t*   onStart: () => console.log('Dash started!'),\n\t\t*   onComplete: () => console.log('Dash finished!')\n\t\t* });\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tdash(direction, additionalSpeed = 4, duration = 200, options) {\n\t\t\tconst totalSpeed = this.speed() + additionalSpeed;\n\t\t\tconst durationSeconds = duration / 1e3;\n\t\t\treturn this.addMovement(new Dash(totalSpeed, direction, durationSeconds), options);\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Apply knockback effect in the specified direction\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Pushes the entity with an initial force that decays over time.\n\t\t* Returns a Promise that resolves when the knockback completes **or is cancelled**.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ## Design notes\n\t\t* - The underlying physics `MovementManager` can cancel strategies via `remove()`, `clear()`,\n\t\t*   or `stopMovement()` **without resolving the Promise** returned by `add()`.\n\t\t* - For this reason, this method considers the knockback finished when either:\n\t\t*   - the `add()` promise resolves (normal completion), or\n\t\t*   - the strategy is no longer present in the active movements list (cancellation).\n\t\t* - When multiple knockbacks overlap, `directionFixed` and `animationFixed` are restored\n\t\t*   only after **all** knockbacks have finished (including cancellations).\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param direction - Normalized direction vector `{ x, y }` for the knockback\n\t\t* @param force - Initial knockback force (default: 5)\n\t\t* @param duration - Duration in milliseconds (default: 300)\n\t\t* @param options - Optional callbacks for movement events\n\t\t* @returns Promise that resolves when the knockback completes or is cancelled\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Simple knockback (await is optional)\n\t\t* await player.knockback({ x: 1, y: 0 }, 5, 300);\n\t\t*\n\t\t* // Overlapping knockbacks: flags are restored only after the last one ends\n\t\t* player.knockback({ x: -1, y: 0 }, 5, 300);\n\t\t* player.knockback({ x: 0, y: 1 }, 3, 200);\n\t\t*\n\t\t* // Cancellation (e.g. map change) will still restore fixed flags\n\t\t* // even if the underlying movement strategy promise is never resolved.\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tasync knockback(direction, force = 5, duration = 300, options) {\n\t\t\tconst durationSeconds = duration / 1e3;\n\t\t\tconst selfAny = this;\n\t\t\tconst lockKey = \"__rpg_knockback_lock__\";\n\t\t\tconst getLock = () => selfAny[lockKey];\n\t\t\tconst setLock = (lock) => {\n\t\t\t\tselfAny[lockKey] = lock;\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tconst clearLock = () => {\n\t\t\t\tdelete selfAny[lockKey];\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tconst hasActiveKnockback = () => this.getActiveMovements().some((s) => s instanceof Knockback || s instanceof AdditiveKnockback);\n\t\t\tconst setAnimationName = (name) => {\n\t\t\t\tif (typeof selfAny.setGraphicAnimation === \"function\") {\n\t\t\t\t\tselfAny.setGraphicAnimation(name);\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tconst animSignal = selfAny.animationName;\n\t\t\t\tif (animSignal && typeof animSignal === \"object\" && typeof animSignal.set === \"function\") animSignal.set(name);\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tconst getAnimationName = () => {\n\t\t\t\tconst animSignal = selfAny.animationName;\n\t\t\t\tif (typeof animSignal === \"function\") try {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn animSignal();\n\t\t\t\t} catch {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tconst restore = () => {\n\t\t\t\tconst lock = getLock();\n\t\t\t\tif (!lock) return;\n\t\t\t\tthis.directionFixed = lock.prevDirectionFixed;\n\t\t\t\tconst prevAnimFixed = lock.prevAnimationFixed;\n\t\t\t\tthis.animationFixed = false;\n\t\t\t\tif (!prevAnimFixed && lock.prevAnimationName) setAnimationName(lock.prevAnimationName);\n\t\t\t\tthis.animationFixed = prevAnimFixed;\n\t\t\t\tclearLock();\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tconst ensureLockInitialized = () => {\n\t\t\t\tif (getLock()) return;\n\t\t\t\tsetLock({\n\t\t\t\t\tprevDirectionFixed: this.directionFixed,\n\t\t\t\t\tprevAnimationFixed: this.animationFixed,\n\t\t\t\t\tprevAnimationName: getAnimationName()\n\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t\tthis.directionFixed = true;\n\t\t\t\tsetAnimationName(\"stand\");\n\t\t\t\tthis.animationFixed = true;\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tconst waitUntilRemovedOrTimeout = (strategy) => {\n\t\t\t\treturn new Promise((resolve) => {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst start = Date.now();\n\t\t\t\t\tconst maxMs = Math.max(0, duration + 1e3);\n\t\t\t\t\tconst intervalId = setInterval(() => {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (!this.getActiveMovements().includes(strategy) || Date.now() - start > maxMs) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tclearInterval(intervalId);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tresolve();\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}, 16);\n\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tensureLockInitialized();\n\t\t\tconst strategy = new AdditiveKnockback(direction, force, durationSeconds);\n\t\t\tconst addPromise = this.addMovement(strategy, options);\n\t\t\ttry {\n\t\t\t\tawait Promise.race([addPromise, waitUntilRemovedOrTimeout(strategy)]);\n\t\t\t} finally {\n\t\t\t\tif (!hasActiveKnockback()) restore();\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Follow a sequence of waypoints\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Makes the entity move through a list of positions at a speed calculated\n\t\t* from the player's base speed. The `speedMultiplier` allows adjusting\n\t\t* the travel speed relative to the player's normal movement speed.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* With default speed=4 and multiplier=0.5: speed = 2 (same as original default)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param waypoints - Array of `{ x, y }` positions to follow in order\n\t\t* @param speedMultiplier - Multiplier applied to base speed (default: 0.5)\n\t\t* @param loop - Whether to loop back to start after reaching the end (default: false)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Follow a patrol path at normal speed\n\t\t* const patrol = [\n\t\t*   { x: 100, y: 100 },\n\t\t*   { x: 200, y: 100 },\n\t\t*   { x: 200, y: 200 }\n\t\t* ];\n\t\t* player.followPath(patrol, 1, true); // Loop at full speed\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Slow walk through waypoints\n\t\t* player.followPath(waypoints, 0.25, false);\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tfollowPath(waypoints, speedMultiplier = .5, loop = false) {\n\t\t\tconst speed = this.speed() * speedMultiplier;\n\t\t\tthis.addMovement(new PathFollow(waypoints, speed, loop));\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Apply oscillating movement pattern\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Creates a back-and-forth movement along the specified axis. The movement\n\t\t* oscillates sinusoidally between -amplitude and +amplitude from the starting position.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param direction - Primary oscillation axis (normalized direction vector)\n\t\t* @param amplitude - Maximum distance from center in pixels (default: 50)\n\t\t* @param period - Time for a complete cycle in milliseconds (default: 2000)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Horizontal oscillation\n\t\t* player.oscillate({ x: 1, y: 0 }, 100, 3000);\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Diagonal bobbing motion\n\t\t* player.oscillate({ x: 1, y: 1 }, 30, 1000);\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\toscillate(direction, amplitude = 50, period = 2e3) {\n\t\t\tthis.addMovement(new Oscillate(direction, amplitude, period));\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Apply ice movement physics\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Simulates slippery surface physics where the entity accelerates gradually\n\t\t* and has difficulty stopping. The maximum speed is based on the player's\n\t\t* base speed multiplied by a speed factor.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* With default speed=4 and factor=1: maxSpeed = 4 (same as original default)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param direction - Target movement direction `{ x, y }`\n\t\t* @param speedFactor - Factor multiplied with base speed for max speed (default: 1.0)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Normal ice physics\n\t\t* player.applyIceMovement({ x: 1, y: 0 });\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Fast ice sliding\n\t\t* player.applyIceMovement({ x: 0, y: 1 }, 1.5);\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tapplyIceMovement(direction, speedFactor = 1) {\n\t\t\tconst maxSpeed = this.speed() * speedFactor;\n\t\t\tthis.addMovement(new IceMovement(direction, maxSpeed));\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Shoot a projectile in the specified direction\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Creates a projectile with ballistic trajectory. The speed is calculated\n\t\t* from the player's base speed multiplied by a speed factor.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* With default speed=4 and factor=50: speed = 200 (same as original default)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param type - Type of projectile trajectory (`Straight`, `Arc`, or `Bounce`)\n\t\t* @param direction - Normalized direction vector `{ x, y }`\n\t\t* @param speedFactor - Factor multiplied with base speed (default: 50)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Straight projectile\n\t\t* player.shootProjectile(ProjectileType.Straight, { x: 1, y: 0 });\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Fast arc projectile\n\t\t* player.shootProjectile(ProjectileType.Arc, { x: 1, y: -0.5 }, 75);\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tshootProjectile(type, direction, speedFactor = 50) {\n\t\t\tconst config = {\n\t\t\t\tspeed: this.speed() * speedFactor,\n\t\t\t\tdirection,\n\t\t\t\tmaxRange: type === ProjectileType.Straight ? 500 : void 0,\n\t\t\t\tmaxHeight: type === ProjectileType.Arc ? 100 : void 0,\n\t\t\t\tgravity: type !== ProjectileType.Straight ? 400 : void 0,\n\t\t\t\tmaxBounces: type === ProjectileType.Bounce ? 3 : void 0,\n\t\t\t\tbounciness: type === ProjectileType.Bounce ? .6 : void 0\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tthis.addMovement(new ProjectileMovement(type, config));\n\t\t}\n\t\tmoveRoutes(routes, options) {\n\t\t\tconst player = this;\n\t\t\tconst selfAny = this;\n\t\t\tconst routeSequence = (selfAny.__moveRouteSequence__ ?? 0) + 1;\n\t\t\tselfAny.__moveRouteSequence__ = routeSequence;\n\t\t\tif (this._finishRoute) {\n\t\t\t\tthis._finishRoute(false);\n\t\t\t\tthis._finishRoute = null;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tthis.clearMovements();\n\t\t\treturn new Promise(async (resolve) => {\n\t\t\t\tthis._finishRoute = resolve;\n\t\t\t\tconst processedRoutes = await Promise.all(routes.map(async (route) => {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (typeof route === \"function\") {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst map = player.getCurrentMap();\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (!map) return;\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn route.apply(route, [player, map]);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn route;\n\t\t\t\t}));\n\t\t\t\tconst finalRoutes = this.flattenRoutes(processedRoutes);\n\t\t\t\tif (selfAny.__moveRouteSequence__ !== routeSequence) {\n\t\t\t\t\tresolve(false);\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tclass RouteMovementStrategy {\n\t\t\t\t\tconstructor(routes, player, onComplete, options) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.routeIndex = options?.stuckThreshold ?? 1;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.processNextRoute();\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tdebugLog(message, data) {}\n\t\t\t\t\tprocessNextRoute() {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.waitingForFrequency = true;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.onComplete(true);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst currentRoute = this.routes[this.routeIndex];\n\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.routeIndex++;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (currentRoute === void 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.processNextRoute();\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttry {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif (typeof currentRoute === \"object\" && \"then\" in (directionStr) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcase \"up\":\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcase Direction.Up:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdirection = Direction.Up;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcase \"down\":\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcase Direction.Down:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdirection = Direction.Down;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcase \"left\":\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcase = Direction.Right;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.debugLog(`TURN to ${directionStr}`);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif (this.player.changeDirection) currentRoute) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdistance += playerSpeed;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.routeIndex++;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t} else break;\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tthis.remainingDistance = ShopGui = class extends Gui {\n\tconstructor(player) {\n\t\tsuper(PrebuiltGui.Shop, player);\n\t\tthis.itemsInput = [];\n\t\tthis.sellMultipliers = {};\n\t\tthis.baseSellMultiplier = (typeof inventory?.price === \"function\" ? inventory.price() : inventory?.price) ?? 0;\n\t\t\t\tconst price = basePrice * multiplier;\n\t\t\t\tif (!basePrice || price <= 0) return;\n\t\t\t\tconst inventory = this.player.getItem?.(id);\n\t\t\t\tif (!inventory) return;\n\t\t\t\tif (inventory.quantity() - nb < 0) return;\n\t\t\t\tthis.player._gold.update((gold) => gold + price * nb);\n\t\t\t\tthis.player.removeItem(id, nb);\n\t\t\t\tthis.player.syncChanges();\n\t\t\t} catch (err) {\n\t\t\t\tconsole.log(err);\n\t\t\t} finally {\n\t\t\t\tthis.refreshShop(clientActionId);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t});\n\t\treturn super.open(this.buildShopData(), {\n\t\t\twaitingAction: true,\n\t\t\tblockPlayerInput: true\n\t\t});\n\t}\n};\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Gui/NotificationGui.ts\nvar NotificationGui = class extends Gui {\n\tconstructor(player) {\n\t\tsuper(PrebuiltGui.Notification, player);\n\t}\n};\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Gui/TitleGui.ts\nvar TitleGui = class extends Gui {\n\tconstructor(player) {\n\t\tsuper(PrebuiltGui.TitleScreen, player);\n\t}\n\topen(options = {}) {\n\t\tthis.on(\"select\", (selection) => {\n\t\t\tthis.close(selection);\n\t\t});\n\t\treturn super.open(options, {\n\t\t\twaitingAction: true,\n\t\t\tblockPlayerInput: true\n\t\t});\n\t}\n};\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Gui/GameoverGui.ts\nvar GameoverGui = class extends Gui {\n\tconstructor(player) {\n\t\tsuper(PrebuiltGui.Gameover, player);\n\t}\n\topen(options = {}) {\n\t\tthis.on(\"select\", (selection) => {\n\t\t\tthis.close(selection);\n\t\t});\n\t\treturn super.open(options, {\n\t\t\twaitingAction: true,\n\t\t\tblockPlayerInput: true\n\t\t});\n\t}\n};\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/GuiManager.ts\n/**\n* GUI Manager Mixin\n*\n* Provides graphical user interface management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n* dialog boxes, menus, notifications, shops, and custom GUI components. It manages the\n* complete GUI system including opening, closing, and data passing between client and server.\n*\n* @param Base - The base class to extend with GUI management\n* @returns Extended class with GUI management methods\n*\n* @example\n* ```ts\n* class MyPlayer extends WithGuiManager(BasePlayer) {\n*   constructor() {\n*     super();\n*     // GUI system is automatically initialized\n*   }\n* }\n*\n* const player = new MyPlayer();\n* await player.showText('Hello World!');\n* player.callMainMenu();\n* ```\n*/\nfunction WithGuiManager(Base) {\n\tclass GuiManagerMixin extends Base {\n\t\tconstructor(..._args) {\n\t\t\tsuper(..._args);\n\t\t\tthis._gui = {};\n\t\t}\n\t\tshowText(msg, options = {}) {\n\t\t\tconst gui = new DialogGui(this);\n\t\t\tthis._gui[gui.id] = choices[indexSelected];\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t\tshowNotification(message, options = {}) {\n\t\t\tthis.emit(\"notification\", {\n\t\t\t\tmessage,\n\t\t\t\t...options\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t\treturn = {}) {\n\t\t\tconst gui = new GameoverGui(this);\n\t\t\tthis._gui[gui.id] = gui;\n\t\t\treturn gui.open(options);\n\t\t}\n\t\tshowSaveLoad(slots = [], options = {}) {\n\t\t\tconst gui = new Opens the GUI named `rpg-shop`\n\t\t*\n\t\t* @title Call Shop Menu\n\t\t* @method player.callShop()\n\t\t* @returns {void}\n\t\t* @memberof GuiManager\n\t\t*/\n\t\tcallShop(items) {\n\t\t\tconst gui = new ShopGui(this);\n\t\t\tthis._gui[gui.id] = gui;\n\t\t\treturn gui.open(items);\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Call a custom Gui\n\t\t\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Calls a client-side component, created with VueJS, named \"inn\".\n\t\t* const gui = player.gui('inn')\n\t\t* \n\t\t*  // You can wait for actions on the menu. It only works if the menu is open.\n\t\t* gui.on('accept', () => {\n\t\t*      player.allRecovery()\n\t\t* })\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // The GUI is opened by passing recoverable data on the client side.\n\t\t* gui.open({ hello: 'world' })\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* When opening the GUI, one can give options\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* await gui.open({ hello: 'world' }, {\n\t\t*      waitingAction: true,\n\t\t*      blockPlayerInput: true\n\t\t* })\n\t\t* // After the GUI is closed\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* - `blockPlayerInput`: while the GUI is open, the player can not move on the map\n\t\t* - `waitingAction`: We explicitly wait until the GUI is closed for the promise to be resolved.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @title Call custom GUI\n\t\t* @method player.gui(guiId)\n\t\t* @param {string} guiId\n\t\t* {\n\t\t\treturn this._gui[guiId];\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Closes the GUI and removes it from memory\n\t\t*\n\t\t* @title Close custom GUI\n\t\t* @method player.removeGui(guiId,data)\n\t\t* @param {string} guiId\n\t\t* @param {object} [data] Passing data if you close the GUI\n\t\t* @returns {Gui}\n\t\t* @memberof GuiManager\n\t\t*/\n\t\tremoveGui(guiId, data) {\n\t\t\tif (this._gui[guiId]) {\n\t\t\t\tthis._gui[guiId].close(data);\n\t\t\t\tdelete this._gui[guiId];\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t_attachedGui(players, display) {\n\t\t\tif (!Array.isArray(players)) players = players\n\t\t*\n\t\t* If you don't specify the players as parameters, it will display the GUI of the instance\n\t\t* But you can specify which GUIs to display by specifying the players as the first parameter\n\t\t*\n\t\t* @title View to GUI attached\n\t\t* @method player.showAttachedGui(players?)\n\t\t* @param {RpgPlayer[] | RpgPlayer} [players] The GUIs attached to the players to display\n\t\t* _players = players || this;\n\t\t\tthis._attachedGui(_players, false);\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn GuiManagerMixin;\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/GoldManager.ts\nfunction WithGoldManager(Base) {\n\treturn class extends Base {\n\t\tset gold(val) {\n\t\t\tif (val < 0) val = 0;\n\t\t\tthis._gold.set(val);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget gold() {\n\t\t\treturn this._gold();\n\t\t}\n\t};\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/VariableManager.ts\n/**\n* Variable Manager Mixin\n* \n* Provides variable management capabilities to any class. Variables are key-value\n* pairs that can store any type of data associated with the player, such as\n* quest progress, game flags, inventory state, and custom game data.\n*\n* Player variables have two main roles:\n*\n* 1. Persist player-specific state so it can be restored from saves.\n* 2. Carry that state across maps and map servers through the player snapshot.\n* \n* @param Base - The base class to extend with variable management\n* @returns Extended class with variable management methods\n* \n* @example\n* ```ts\n* class MyPlayer extends WithVariableManager(BasePlayer) {\n*   constructor() {\n*     super();\n*     // Variables are automatically initialized\n*   }\n* }\n* \n* const player = new MyPlayer();\n* player.setVariable('questCompleted', true);\n* ```\n*/\nfunction WithVariableManager(Base) {\n\treturn class extends Base {\n\t\tconstructor(..._args) {\n\t\t\tsuper(..._args);\n\t\t\tthis.variables = type(signal({}), \"variables\", { persist: true }, this);\n\t\t}\n\t\tsetVariable(key, val) {\n\t\t\tthis.variables.mutate((variables) => {\n\t\t\t\tvariables[key] = val;\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t\tgetVariable(key) {\n\t\t\treturn this.variables()[key];\n\t\t}\n\t\tremoveVariable(key) {\n\t\t\tif (!(key in this.variables())) return false;\n\t\t\tthis.variables.mutate((draft) => {\n\t\t\t\tdelete draft[key];\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t\treturn true;\n\t\t}\n\t\thasVariable(key) {\n\t\t\treturn key in this.variables();\n\t\t}\n\t\tgetVariableKeys() {\n\t\t\treturn Object.keys(this.variables());\n\t\t}\n\t\tclearVariables() {\n\t\t\tthis.variables.set({});\n\t\t}\n\t};\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/ParameterManager.ts\nvar DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE = {\n\tbasis: 30,\n\textra: 20,\n\taccelerationA: 30,\n\taccelerationB: 30\n};\nfunction isObject$1(value) {\n\treturn typeof value === \"object\" && value !== null;\n}\nfunction toValidNumber(value, fallback) {\n\treturn typeof value === \"number\" && Number.isFinite(value) ? value : fallback;\n}\nfunction normalizeExpCurve(value) {\n\tif (!isObject$1(value)) return DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE;\n\treturn {\n\t\tbasis: toValidNumber(value.basis, DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE.basis),\n\t\textra: toValidNumber(value.extra, DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE.extra),\n\t\taccelerationA: toValidNumber(value.accelerationA, DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE.accelerationA),\n\t\taccelerationB: toValidNumber(value.accelerationB, DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE.accelerationB)\n\t};\n}\nfunction normalizeParameterCurve(value) {\n\tif (typeof value === \"number\") {\n\t\tconst normalized = toValidNumber(value, 0);\n\t\treturn {\n\t\t\tstart: normalized,\n\t\t\tend: normalized\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\tif (!isObject$1(value)) return {\n\t\tstart: 0,\n\t\tend: 0\n\t};\n\tconst start = toValidNumber(value.start, 0);\n\treturn {\n\t\tstart,\n\t\tend: toValidNumber(value.end, start)\n\t};\n}\n/**\n* Parameter Manager Mixin with Reactive Signals\n* \n* Provides comprehensive parameter management functionality using reactive signals from `@signe/reactive`.\n* This mixin handles health points (HP), skill points (SP), experience and level progression, \n* custom parameters, and parameter modifiers with automatic reactivity.\n* \n* **Key Features:**\n* - ✨ **Reactive Parameters**: All parameters automatically recalculate when level or modifiers change\n* - 🚀 **Performance Optimized**: Uses computed signals to avoid unnecessary recalculations  \n* - 🔄 **Real-time Updates**: Changes propagate automatically throughout the system\n* - 🎯 **Type Safe**: Full TypeScript support with proper type inference\n* \n* @template TBase - The base class constructor type\n* @param Base - The base class to extend with parameter management\n* @returns Extended class with reactive parameter management methods\n* \n* @example\n* ```ts\n* class MyPlayer extends WithParameterManager(BasePlayer) {\n*   constructor() {\n*     super();\n*     \n*     // Add custom parameters\n*     this.addParameter('strength', { start: 10, end: 100 });\n*     this.addParameter('magic', { start: 5, end: 80 });\n*   }\n* }\n* \n* const player = new MyPlayer();\n* \n* // Reactive parameter updates\n* player.level = 5;\n* console.log(player.param.strength); // Automatically calculated for level 5\n* \n* // Reactive modifiers\n* player.paramsModifier = {\n*   [MAXHP]: { value: 100, rate: 1.2 }\n* };\n* console.log(player.param[MAXHP]); // Automatically includes modifiers\n* ```\n*/\nfunction WithParameterManager(Base) {\n\treturn class extends Base {\n\t\tconstructor(..._args) {\n\t\t\tsuper(..._args);\n\t\t\tthis._paramsModifierSignal = type(signal({}), \"_paramsModifierSignal\", { persist: true }, this);\n\t\t\tthis._parametersSignal = type(signal({}), \"_parametersSignal\", { persist: true }, this);\n\t\t\tthis._paramProxy = null;\n\t\t\tthis._param = type(computed(() => {\n\t\t\t\tconst obj = {};\n\t\t\t\tconst parameters = this._parametersSignal();\n\t\t\t\tconst allModifiers = this._getAggregatedModifiers();\n\t\t\t\tconst level = this._level();\n\t\t\t\tfor (const [name, paramConfig] of Object.entries(parameters)) {\n\t\t\t\t\tlet curveVal = Math.floor((paramConfig.end - paramConfig.start) * ((level - 1) / (this.finalLevel - this.initialLevel))) + paramConfig.start;\n\t\t\t\t\tconst modifier = allModifiers[name];\n\t\t\t\t\tif (modifier) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (modifier.rate) curveVal *= modifier.rate;\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (modifier.value) curveVal += modifier.value;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tobj[name] = curveVal;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn obj;\n\t\t\t}), \"_param\", {}, this);\n\t\t\tthis.initialLevel = 1;\n\t\t\tthis.finalLevel = 99;\n\t\t\tthis._expCurveSignal = type(signal(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE)), \"_expCurveSignal\", { persist: true }, this);\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Aggregates parameter modifiers from all sources (direct modifiers, states, equipment)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* This method combines modifiers from multiple sources and calculates the final\n\t\t* modifier values for each parameter. It handles both value and rate modifiers.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @returns Aggregated parameter modifiers\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Internal usage - gets modifiers from all sources\n\t\t* const modifiers = this._getAggregatedModifiers();\n\t\t* console.log(modifiers[MAXHP]); // { value: 100, rate: 1.2 }\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\t_getAggregatedModifiers() {\n\t\t\tconst params = {};\n\t\t\tconst paramsAvg = {};\n\t\t\tconst changeParam = (paramsModifier) => {\n\t\t\t\tfor (let key in paramsModifier) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst { rate, value } = paramsModifier[key];\n\t\t\t\t\tif (!params[key]) params[key] = {\n\t\t\t\t\t\trate: 0,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tvalue: 0\n\t\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t\t\tif (!paramsAvg[key]) paramsAvg[key] = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\tif (value) params[key].value += value;\n\t\t\t\t\tif (rate !== void 0) params[key].rate += rate;\n\t\t\t\t\tparamsAvg[key]++;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tconst getModifier = (prop) => {\n\t\t\t\tif (!isString(prop)) {\n\t\t\t\t\tchangeParam(prop);\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfor (let el of this[prop]()) {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (!el.paramsModifier) continue;\n\t\t\t\t\tchangeParam(el.paramsModifier);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tgetModifier(this._paramsModifierSignal());\n\t\t\tgetModifier(\"states\");\n\t\t\tgetModifier(\"equipments\");\n\t\t\tfor (let key in params) params[key].rate /= paramsAvg[key];\n\t\t\treturn params;\n\t\t}\n\t\tget expCurve() {\n\t\t\tconst raw = this._expCurveSignal();\n\t\t\tif (!raw) return DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE;\n\t\t\ttry {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizeExpCurve(JSON.parse(raw));\n\t\t\t} catch {\n\t\t\t\treturn DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tset expCurve(val) {\n\t\t\tthis._expCurveSignal.set(JSON.stringify(val));\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* Changes the health points\n\t\t* - Cannot exceed the MaxHP parameter\n\t\t* - Cannot have a negative value\n\t\t* - If the value is 0, a hook named `onDead()` is called in the RpgPlayer class.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* player.hp = 100\n\t\t* ``` \n\t\t* @title Change HP\n\t\t* @prop {number} player.hp\n\t\t* @default MaxHPValue\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\tset hp(val) {\n\t\t\tif (val > this.param[\"maxHp\"]) val = this.param[MAXHP];\n\t\t\telse if (val <= 0) {\n\t\t\t\tthis[\"execMethod\"](\"onDead\");\n\t\t\t\tval = 0;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tthis.hpSignal.set(val);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget hp() {\n\t\t\treturn this.hpSignal();\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* Changes the skill points\n\t\t* - Cannot exceed the MaxSP parameter\n\t\t* - Cannot have a negative value\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* player.sp = 200\n\t\t* ``` \n\t\t* @title Change SP\n\t\t* @prop {number} player.sp\n\t\t* @default MaxSPValue\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\tset sp(val) {\n\t\t\tif (val > this.param[\"maxSp\"]) val = this.param[MAXSP];\n\t\t\tthis.spSignal.set(val);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget sp() {\n\t\t\treturn this.spSignal();\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* Changing the player's experience. \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* player.exp += 100\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Levels are based on the experience curve.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* console.log(player.level) // 1\n\t\t* console.log(player.expForNextlevel) // 150\n\t\t* player.exp += 160\n\t\t* console.log(player.level) // 2\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @title Change Experience\n\t\t* @prop {number} player.exp\n\t\t* @default 0\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\tset exp(val) {\n\t\t\tthis._exp.set(val);\n\t\t\tthis.level;\n\t\t\twhile (this.expForNextlevel < this._exp()) this.level += 1;\n\t\t}\n\t\tget exp() {\n\t\t\treturn this._exp();\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* Changing the player's level. \n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* player.level += 1\n\t\t* ``` \n\t\t* \n\t\t* The level will be between the initial level given by the `initialLevel` and final level given by `finalLevel`\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* player.finalLevel = 50\n\t\t* player.level = 60 \n\t\t* console.log(player.level) // 50\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @title Change Level\n\t\t* @prop {number} player.level\n\t\t* @default 1\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\tset level(val) {\n\t\t\tconst lastLevel = this._level();\n\t\t\tif (this.finalLevel && val > this.finalLevel) val = this.finalLevel;\n\t\t\tconst currentClass = this._class && this._class();\n\t\t\tif (currentClass && \"skillsToLearn\" in currentClass && Array.isArray(currentClass.skillsToLearn)) {\n\t\t\t\tfor (let i = this._level(); i <= val; i++) for (let skill of currentClass.skillsToLearn) if (skill.level == i) this[\"learnSkill\"](skill.skill);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tconst hasNewLevel = val - lastLevel;\n\t\t\tif (hasNewLevel > 0) this[\"execMethod\"](\"onLevelUp\", [hasNewLevel]);\n\t\t\tthis._level.set(val);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget level() {\n\t\t\treturn this._level();\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* console.log(player.expForNextlevel) // 150\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* @title Experience for next level ?\n\t\t* @prop {number} player.expForNextlevel\n\t\t* @readonly\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\tget expForNextlevel() {\n\t\t\treturn this._expForLevel(this.level + 1);\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* Read the value of a parameter. Put the name of the parameter.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n\t\t* \n\t\t* const { MAXHP } = Presets \n\t\t* \n\t\t* console.log(player.param[MAXHP])\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* > Possible to use the `player.getParamValue(name)` method instead\n\t\t* @title Get Param Value\n\t\t* @prop {object} player.param\n\t\t* @readonly\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\tget param() {\n\t\t\tif (!this._paramProxy) this._paramProxy = new Proxy({}, {\n\t\t\t\tget: (_target, property) => {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (typeof property !== \"string\") return;\n\t\t\t\t\treturn this._param()[property];\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tset: (_target, property, value) => {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (typeof property !== \"string\") return false;\n\t\t\t\t\tthis.setParameter(property, value);\n\t\t\t\t\treturn true;\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\thas: (_target, property) => {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (typeof property !== \"string\") return false;\n\t\t\t\t\treturn property in this._param();\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\townKeys: () => Reflect.ownKeys(this._param()),\n\t\t\t\tgetOwnPropertyDescriptor: (_target, property) => {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (typeof property !== \"string\") return;\n\t\t\t\t\tconst parameters = this._param();\n\t\t\t\t\tif (!(property in parameters)) return;\n\t\t\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconfigurable: true,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tenumerable: true,\n\t\t\t\t\t\twritable: true,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tvalue: parameters[property]\n\t\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t\treturn this._paramProxy;\n\t\t}\n\t\tget paramsModifier() {\n\t\t\treturn this._paramsModifierSignal();\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* Changes the values of some parameters\n\t\t* \n\t\t* > It is important that these parameters have been created beforehand with the `addParameter()` method.\n\t\t* > By default, the following settings have been created: \n\t\t* - maxhp\n\t\t* - maxsp\n\t\t* - str\n\t\t* - int\n\t\t* - dex\n\t\t* - agi\n\t\t* \n\t\t* **Object Key**\n\t\t* \n\t\t* The key of the object is the name of the parameter\n\t\t* \n\t\t* > The good practice is to retrieve the name coming from a constant\n\t\t* \n\t\t* **Object Value**\n\t\t* \n\t\t* The value of the key is an object containing: \n\t\t* ``` \n\t\t* {\n\t\t*   value: number,\n\t\t*   rate: number\n\t\t* }\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* - value: Adds a number to the parameter\n\t\t* - rate: Adds a rate to the parameter\n\t\t* \n\t\t* > Note that you can put both (value and rate)\n\t\t* \n\t\t* In the case of a state or the equipment of a weapon or armor, the parameters will be changed but if the state disappears or the armor/weapon is de-equipped, then the parameters will return to the initial state.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @prop {Object} [paramsModifier]\n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n\t\t* \n\t\t* const { MAXHP } = Presets\n\t\t* \n\t\t* player.paramsModifier = {\n\t\t*      [MAXHP]: {\n\t\t*          value: 100\n\t\t*      }\n\t\t* }\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* 1. Player has 741 MaxHp\n\t\t* 2. After changing the parameter, he will have 841 MaxHp\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @title Set Parameters Modifier\n\t\t* @prop {number} paramsModifier\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\tset paramsModifier(val) {\n\t\t\tthis._paramsModifierSignal.set(val);\n\t\t}\n\t\tget parameters() {\n\t\t\treturn this._parametersSignal();\n\t\t}\n\t\tset parameters(val) {\n\t\t\tconst normalizedParameters = {};\n\t\t\tfor (const [name, parameterValue] of Object.entries(val || {})) normalizedParameters[name] = normalizeParameterCurve(parameterValue);\n\t\t\tthis._parametersSignal.set(normalizedParameters);\n\t\t\tif (\"maxHp\" in normalizedParameters && this.hp > this.param[\"maxHp\"]) this.hp = this.param[MAXHP];\n\t\t\tif (\"maxSp\" in normalizedParameters && this.sp > this.param[\"maxSp\"]) this.sp = this.param[MAXSP];\n\t\t}\n\t\t_expForLevel(level) {\n\t\t\tconst { basis, extra, accelerationA, accelerationB } = this.expCurve;\n\t\t\treturn Math.round(basis * Math.pow(level - 1, .9 + accelerationA / 250) * level * (level + 1) / (6 + Math.pow(level, 2) / 50 / accelerationB) + (level - 1) * extra);\n\t\t}\n\t\tgetParamValue(name) {\n\t\t\treturn this.param[name];\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* Give a new parameter. Give a start value and an end value. \n\t\t* The start value will be set to the level set at `player.initialLevel` and the end value will be linked to the level set at `player.finalLevel`.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* const SPEED = 'speed'\n\t\t* \n\t\t* player.addParameter(SPEED, {\n\t\t*     start: 10,\n\t\t*     end: 100\n\t\t* })\n\t\t* \n\t\t* player.param[SPEED] // 10\n\t\t* player.level += 5\n\t\t* player.param[SPEED] // 14\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @title Add custom parameters\n\t\t* @method player.addParameter(name,curve)\n\t\t* @param {string} name - The name of the parameter\n\t\t* @param {object} curve - Scheme of the object: { start: number, end: number }\n\t\t* @returns {void}\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\tsetParameter(name, value) {\n\t\t\tconst normalizedValue = normalizeParameterCurve(value);\n\t\t\tthis._parametersSignal.mutate((parameters) => {\n\t\t\t\tparameters[name] = {\n\t\t\t\t\tstart: normalizedValue.start,\n\t\t\t\t\tend: normalizedValue.end\n\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t\tconst maxHp = this.param[MAXHP];\n\t\t\tconst maxSp = this.param[MAXSP];\n\t\t\tif (name == \"maxHp\" && this.hp > maxHp) this.hp = maxHp;\n\t\t\telse if (name == \"maxSp\" && this.sp > maxSp) this.sp = maxSp;\n\t\t}\n\t\taddParameter(name, { start, end }) {\n\t\t\tthis.setParameter(name, {\n\t\t\t\tstart,\n\t\t\t\tend\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* Gives back in percentage of health points to skill points\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n\t\t* \n\t\t* const { MAXHP } = Presets \n\t\t* \n\t\t* console.log(player.param[MAXHP]) // 800\n\t\t* player.hp = 100\n\t\t* player.recovery({ hp: 0.5 }) // = 800 * 0.5\n\t\t* console.log(player.hp) // 400\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @title Recovery HP and/or SP\n\t\t* @method player.recovery(params)\n\t\t* @param {object} params - Scheme of the object: { hp: number, sp: number }. The values of the numbers must be in 0 and 1\n\t\t* @returns {void}\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\trecovery({ hp, sp }) {\n\t\t\tif (hp) this.hp = this.param[MAXHP] * hp;\n\t\t\tif (sp) this.sp = this.param[MAXSP] * sp;\n\t\t}\n\t\t/** \n\t\t* restores all HP and SP\n\t\t* \n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n\t\t* \n\t\t* const { MAXHP, MAXSP } = Presets \n\t\t* \n\t\t* console.log(player.param[MAXHP], player.param[MAXSP]) // 800, 230\n\t\t* player.hp = 100\n\t\t* player.sp = 0\n\t\t* player.allRecovery()\n\t\t* console.log(player.hp, player.sp) // 800, 230\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @title All Recovery\n\t\t* @method player.allRecovery()\n\t\t* @returns {void}\n\t\t* @memberof ParameterManager\n\t\t* */\n\t\tallRecovery() {\n\t\t\tthis.recovery({\n\t\t\t\thp: 1,\n\t\t\t\tsp: 1\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t};\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/ItemFixture.ts\nfunction WithItemFixture(Base) {\n\treturn class extends Base {\n\t\tgetFeature(name, prop) {\n\t\t\tconst array = {};\n\t\t\tfor (let item of this.equipments()) if (item[name]) for (let feature of item[name]) {\n\t\t\t\tconst { rate } = feature;\n\t\t\t\tconst instance = feature[prop];\n\t\t\t\tconst cache = array[instance.id];\n\t\t\t\tif (cache && cache.rate >= rate) continue;\n\t\t\t\tarray[instance.id] = feature;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn Object.values(array);\n\t\t}\n\t};\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/logs/log.ts\nvar Log = class extends Error {\n\tconstructor(id, msg) {\n\t\tsuper(`[${id}] ${msg}`);\n\t\tthis.name = \"RpgLog\";\n\t\tthis.id = id;\n\t\tObject.setPrototypeOf(this, new.target.prototype);\n\t}\n};\n//#endregion\n//#region src/logs/item.ts\nvar ItemLog = class {\n\tstatic notInInventory(itemClass)
