{\n\t\t\tthis._elementsEfficiency = val;\n\t\t}\n\t\tget elements() {\n\t\t\tlet elements = [];\n\t\t\tfor (let item of this.equipments()) if (item.elements) elements = [...elements, ...item.elements];\n\t\t\treturn arrayUniq(elements);\n\t\t}\n\t\tcoefficientElements(otherPlayer) {\n\t\t\tconst atkPlayerElements = otherPlayer.elements;\n\t\t\tconst playerElements = this.elementsEfficiency;\n\t\t\tlet coefficient = 1;\n\t\t\tfor (let atkElement of atkPlayerElements) {\n\t\t\t\tconst elementPlayer = playerElements.find((el) => el.element == atkElement.element);\n\t\t\t\tconst elementPlayerDef = this.elementsDefense.find((el) => el.element == atkElement.element);\n\t\t\t\tif (!elementPlayer) continue;\n\t\t\t\tconst fn = this.getFormulas(\"coefficientElements\");\n\t\t\t\tif (!fn) return coefficient;\n\t\t\t\tcoefficient += fn(atkElement, elementPlayer, elementPlayerDef || { rate: 0 });\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn coefficient;\n\t\t}\n\t};\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/SkillManager.ts\n/**\n* Skill Manager Mixin\n* \n* Provides skill management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n* learning, forgetting, and using skills, including SP cost management,\n* hit rate calculations, and skill effects application.\n* \n* Supports three input formats for skills:\n* - **String ID**: References a skill in the database\n* - **Class**: A skill class that will be instantiated\n* - **Object**: A skill object with properties and hooks\n* \n* @param Base - The base class to extend with skill management\n* @returns Extended class with skill management methods\n* \n* @example\n* ```ts\n* // Using string ID (from database)\n* player.learnSkill('fire');\n* \n* // Using skill class\n* player.learnSkill(FireSkill);\n* \n* // Using skill object\n* player.learnSkill({\n*   id: 'ice',\n*   name: 'Ice',\n*   spCost: 15,\n*   onUse(player) {\n*     console.log('Ice spell cast!');\n*   }\n* });\n* ```\n*/\nfunction WithSkillManager(Base) {\n\treturn class extends Base {\n\t\t_getSkillSnapshot(skillData) {\n\t\t\tif (!skillData) return null;\n\t\t\tconst snapshot = { ...skillData._skillData || skillData };\n\t\t\tfor (const key of [\n\t\t\t\t\"id\",\n\t\t\t\t\"name\",\n\t\t\t\t\"description\",\n\t\t\t\t\"spCost\",\n\t\t\t\t\"icon\",\n\t\t\t\t\"hitRate\",\n\t\t\t\t\"power\",\n\t\t\t\t\"coefficient\"\n\t\t\t]) {\n\t\t\t\tconst value = skillData[key];\n\t\t\t\tif (typeof value === \"function\") {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (key === \"hitRate\" && !(key in snapshot)) continue;\n\t\t\t\t\tsnapshot[key] = value();\n\t\t\t\t} else if (value !== void 0) snapshot[key] = value;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (skillData._skillInstance) snapshot._skillInstance = skillData._skillInstance;\n\t\t\treturn snapshot;\n\t\t}\n\t\t_getLearnedSkillEntry(skillInput) {\n\t\t\tconst index = this._getSkillIndex(skillInput);\n\t\t\treturn index >= 0 ? this.skills()[index] : null;\n\t\t}\n\t\t_getSkillMap(required = true) {\n\t\t\tconst map = this.getCurrentMap?.() || this.map;\n\t\t\tif (required && (!map || !map.database)) throw new Error(\"Player must be on a map to learn skills\");\n\t\t\treturn map;\n\t\t}\n\t\t_resolveSkillInput(skillInput, map, databaseByIdOverride) {\n\t\t\tlet skillId = \"\";\n\t\t\tlet skillData;\n\t\t\tlet skillInstance = null;\n\t\t\tif (isString(skillInput)) {\n\t\t\t\tskillId = skillInput;\n\t\t\t\tskillData = databaseByIdOverride ? databaseByIdOverride(skillId) : this.databaseById(skillId);\n\t\t\t} else if (typeof skillInput === \"function\") {\n\t\t\t\tconst SkillClassCtor = skillInput;\n\t\t\t\tskillId = SkillClassCtor.id || SkillClassCtor.name;\n\t\t\t\tconst existingData = map?.database()?.[skillId];\n\t\t\t\tif (existingData) skillData = existingData;\n\t\t\t\telse if (map) {\n\t\t\t\t\tmap.addInDatabase(skillId, SkillClassCtor);\n\t\t\t\t\tskillData = SkillClassCtor;\n\t\t\t\t} else skillData = SkillClassCtor;\n\t\t\t\tskillInstance = new SkillClassCtor();\n\t\t\t\tskillData = {\n\t\t\t\t\t...skillData,\n\t\t\t\t\t...skillInstance,\n\t\t\t\t\tid: skillId\n\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tconst skillObj = skillInput;\n\t\t\t\tskillId = skillObj.id || `skill-${Date.now()}`;\n\t\t\t\tskillObj.id = skillId;\n\t\t\t\tconst existingData = map?.database()?.[skillId];\n\t\t\t\tif (existingData) {\n\t\t\t\t\tskillData = {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t...existingData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t...skillObj\n\t\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t\t\tif (map) map.addInDatabase(skillId, skillData, { force: true });\n\t\t\t\t} else if (map) {\n\t\t\t\t\tmap.addInDatabase(skillId, skillObj);\n\t\t\t\t\tskillData = skillObj;\n\t\t\t\t} else skillData = skillObj;\n\t\t\t\tskillInstance = skillObj;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\tskillId,\n\t\t\t\tskillData,\n\t\t\t\tskillInstance\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t}\n\t\t_createSkillInstance(skillId, skillData, skillInstance) {\n\t\t\tconst instance = new Skill(skillData);\n\t\t\tinstance.id.set(skillId);\n\t\t\tinstance._skillData = {\n\t\t\t\t...skillData,\n\t\t\t\tid: skillId\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tif (skillInstance) instance._skillInstance = skillInstance;\n\t\t\treturn instance;\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Create a skill instance without learning side effects.\n\t\t*/\n\t\tcreateSkillInstance(skillInput) {\n\t\t\tconst map = this._getSkillMap();\n\t\t\tconst { skillId, skillData, skillInstance } = this._resolveSkillInput(skillInput, map);\n\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\tskillId,\n\t\t\t\tskillData,\n\t\t\t\tskillInstance,\n\t\t\t\tinstance: this._createSkillInstance(skillId, skillData, skillInstance)\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Resolve skill snapshot entries into Skill instances without side effects.\n\t\t*/\n\t\tresolveSkillsSnapshot(snapshot, mapOverride) {\n\t\t\tif (!snapshot || !Array.isArray(snapshot.skills)) return snapshot;\n\t\t\tconst map = mapOverride ?? this._getSkillMap(false);\n\t\t\tif (!map || !map.database) return snapshot;\n\t\t\tconst databaseByIdOverride = (id) => {\n\t\t\t\tconst data = map.database()[id];\n\t\t\t\tif (!data) throw new Error(`The ID=${id} data is not found in the database. Add the data in the property \"database\"`);\n\t\t\t\treturn data;\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tconst skills = snapshot.skills.map((entry) => {\n\t\t\t\tconst skillId = isString(entry) ? entry : entry?.id;\n\t\t\t\tif (!skillId) return entry;\n\t\t\t\tconst { skillData, skillInstance } = this._resolveSkillInput(skillId, map, databaseByIdOverride);\n\t\t\t\treturn this._createSkillInstance(skillId, skillData, skillInstance);\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\t...snapshot,\n\t\t\t\tskills\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Find the index of a skill in the skills array\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Searches by ID for both string inputs and object/class inputs.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to find\n\t\t* @returns Index of the skill or -1 if not found\n\t\t*/\n\t\t_getSkillIndex(skillInput) {\n\t\t\tlet searchId = \"\";\n\t\t\tif (isString(skillInput)) searchId = skillInput;\n\t\t\telse if (typeof skillInput === \"function\") searchId = skillInput.id || skillInput.name;\n\t\t\telse searchId = skillInput.id || \"\";\n\t\t\treturn this.skills().findIndex((skill) => {\n\t\t\t\treturn (skill.id() || skill.name() || \"\") === searchId;\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Retrieves a learned skill\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Searches the player's learned skills by ID, class, or object.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to find\n\t\t* @returns The skill data if found, null otherwise\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* const skill = player.getSkill('fire');\n\t\t* if (skill) {\n\t\t*   console.log(`Fire skill costs ${skill.spCost} SP`);\n\t\t* }\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tgetSkill(skillInput) {\n\t\t\tconst skill = this._getLearnedSkillEntry(skillInput);\n\t\t\treturn this._getSkillSnapshot(skill);\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Learn a new skill\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Adds a skill to the player's skill list. Supports three input formats:\n\t\t* - **String ID**: Retrieves the skill from the database\n\t\t* - **Class**: Creates an instance and adds to database if needed\n\t\t* - **Object**: Uses directly and adds to database if needed\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to learn\n\t\t* @returns The learned skill data\n\t\t* @throws SkillLog.alreadyLearned if the skill is already known\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // From database\n\t\t* player.learnSkill('fire');\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // From class\n\t\t* player.learnSkill(FireSkill);\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // From object\n\t\t* player.learnSkill({\n\t\t*   id: 'custom-skill',\n\t\t*   name: 'Custom Skill',\n\t\t*   spCost: 20,\n\t\t*   onLearn(player) {\n\t\t*     console.log('Learned custom skill!');\n\t\t*   }\n\t\t* });\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tlearnSkill(skillInput) {\n\t\t\tconst map = this._getSkillMap();\n\t\t\tconst { skillId, skillData, skillInstance } = this._resolveSkillInput(skillInput, map);\n\t\t\tif (this._getLearnedSkillEntry(skillId)) throw SkillLog.alreadyLearned(skillData);\n\t\t\tconst instance = this._createSkillInstance(skillId, skillData, skillInstance);\n\t\t\tthis.skills().push(instance);\n\t\t\tconst hookTarget = instance._skillInstance || instance;\n\t\t\tthis[\"execMethod\"](\"onLearn\", [this], hookTarget);\n\t\t\treturn skillData;\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Forget a learned skill\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Removes a skill from the player's skill list.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to forget\n\t\t* @returns The forgotten skill data\n\t\t* @throws SkillLog.notLearned if the skill is not known\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* player.forgetSkill('fire');\n\t\t* // or\n\t\t* player.forgetSkill(FireSkill);\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tforgetSkill(skillInput) {\n\t\t\tconst index = this._getSkillIndex(skillInput);\n\t\t\tif (index === -1) {\n\t\t\t\tlet skillData = skillInput;\n\t\t\t\tif (isString(skillInput)) try {\n\t\t\t\t\tskillData = this.databaseById(skillInput);\n\t\t\t\t} catch {\n\t\t\t\t\tskillData = {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tname: skillInput,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tid: skillInput\n\t\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\telse if (typeof skillInput === \"function\") skillData = {\n\t\t\t\t\tname: skillInput.name,\n\t\t\t\t\tid: skillInput.id || skillInput.name\n\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t\tthrow SkillLog.notLearned(skillData);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tconst skillEntry = this.skills()[index];\n\t\t\tconst skillData = this._getSkillSnapshot(skillEntry);\n\t\t\tthis.skills().splice(index, 1);\n\t\t\tconst hookTarget = skillEntry?._skillInstance || skillEntry?._skillData || skillData;\n\t\t\tthis[\"execMethod\"](\"onForget\", [this], hookTarget);\n\t\t\treturn skillData;\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Use a learned skill\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Executes a skill, consuming SP and applying effects to targets.\n\t\t* The skill must be learned and the player must have enough SP.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to use\n\t\t* @param otherPlayer - Optional target player(s) to apply skill effects to\n\t\t* @returns The used skill data\n\t\t* @throws SkillLog.restriction if player has CAN_NOT_SKILL effect\n\t\t* @throws SkillLog.notLearned if skill is not known\n\t\t* @throws SkillLog.notEnoughSp if not enough SP\n\t\t* @throws SkillLog.chanceToUseFailed if hit rate check fails\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Use skill without target\n\t\t* player.useSkill('fire');\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Use skill on a target\n\t\t* player.useSkill('fire', enemy);\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Use skill on multiple targets\n\t\t* player.useSkill('fire', [enemy1, enemy2]);\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tuseSkill(skillInput, otherPlayer) {\n\t\t\tconst skillEntry = this._getLearnedSkillEntry(skillInput);\n\t\t\tconst skill = this._getSkillSnapshot(skillEntry);\n\t\t\tif (this.hasEffect(Effect.CAN_NOT_SKILL)) throw SkillLog.restriction(skill || skillInput);\n\t\t\tif (!skill) throw SkillLog.notLearned(skillInput);\n\t\t\tconst spCost = typeof skill.spCost === \"number\" ? skill.spCost : 0;\n\t\t\tif (spCost > this.sp) throw SkillLog.notEnoughSp(skill, spCost, this.sp);\n\t\t\tconst costMultiplier = this.hasEffect(Effect.HALF_SP_COST) ? 2 : 1;\n\t\t\tthis.sp -= spCost / costMultiplier;\n\t\t\tconst hitRate = typeof skill.hitRate === \"number\" ? skill.hitRate : 1;\n\t\t\tif (Math.random() > hitRate) {\n\t\t\t\tconst hookTarget = skillEntry?._skillInstance || skillEntry?._skillData || skill;\n\t\t\t\tthis[\"execMethod\"](\"onUseFailed\", [this, otherPlayer], hookTarget);\n\t\t\t\tthrow SkillLog.chanceToUseFailed(skill);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (otherPlayer) {\n\t\t\t\tconst players = isArray(otherPlayer) ? otherPlayer : [otherPlayer];\n\t\t\t\tfor (const player of players) {\n\t\t\t\t\tthis.applyStates(player, skill);\n\t\t\t\t\tplayer.applyDamage(this, skill);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tconst hookTarget = skillEntry?._skillInstance || skillEntry?._skillData || skill;\n\t\t\tthis[\"execMethod\"](\"onUse\", [this, otherPlayer], hookTarget);\n\t\t\treturn skill;\n\t\t}\n\t};\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/BattleManager.ts\nfunction WithBattleManager(Base) {\n\treturn class extends Base {\n\t\tapplyDamage(attackerPlayer, skill) {\n\t\t\tconst self = this;\n\t\t\tconst getParam = (player) => {\n\t\t\t\tconst params = {};\n\t\t\t\tObject.keys(self.parameters).forEach((key) => {\n\t\t\t\t\tparams[key] = player.param[key];\n\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\t\t[\"atk\"]: player.atk,\n\t\t\t\t\t[PDEF]: player.pdef,\n\t\t\t\t\t[SDEF]: player.sdef,\n\t\t\t\t\t...params\n\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tlet damage = 0, fn;\n\t\t\tlet critical = false;\n\t\t\tlet guard = false;\n\t\t\tlet superGuard = false;\n\t\t\tlet elementVulnerable = false;\n\t\t\tconst paramA = getParam(attackerPlayer);\n\t\t\tconst paramB = getParam(self);\n\t\t\tif (skill) {\n\t\t\t\tfn = self.getFormulas(\"damageSkill\");\n\t\t\t\tif (!fn) throw new Error(\"Skill Formulas not exists\");\n\t\t\t\tdamage = fn(paramA, paramB, skill);\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tfn = self.getFormulas(\"damagePhysic\");\n\t\t\t\tif (!fn) throw new Error(\"Physic Formulas not exists\");\n\t\t\t\tdamage = fn(paramA, paramB);\n\t\t\t\tconst coef = self.coefficientElements(attackerPlayer);\n\t\t\t\tif (coef >= 2) elementVulnerable = true;\n\t\t\t\tdamage *= coef;\n\t\t\t\tfn = self.getFormulas(\"damageCritical\");\n\t\t\t\tif (fn) {\n\t\t\t\t\tlet newDamage = fn(damage, paramA, paramB);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (damage != newDamage) critical = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tdamage = newDamage;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (self.hasEffect(Effect.GUARD)) {\n\t\t\t\tfn = self.getFormulas(\"damageGuard\");\n\t\t\t\tif (fn) {\n\t\t\t\t\tlet newDamage = fn(damage, paramA, paramB);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (damage != newDamage) guard = true;\n\t\t\t\t\tdamage = newDamage;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (self.hasEffect(Effect.SUPER_GUARD)) {\n\t\t\t\tdamage /= 4;\n\t\t\t\tsuperGuard = true;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tself.hp -= damage;\n\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\tdamage,\n\t\t\t\tcritical,\n\t\t\t\telementVulnerable,\n\t\t\t\tguard,\n\t\t\t\tsuperGuard\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Get damage formulas from the current map\n\t\t* \n\t\t* Retrieves the damage calculation formulas defined in the current map's configuration.\n\t\t* These formulas are used to calculate different types of damage including physical,\n\t\t* magical, critical hits, and guard effects. The formulas provide flexibility in\n\t\t* customizing the battle system's damage calculations.\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @param name - The name of the formula to retrieve (e.g., 'damagePhysic', 'damageSkill')\n\t\t* @returns The formula function or undefined if not found\n\t\t* \n\t\t* @example\n\t\t* ```ts\n\t\t* // Get physical damage formula\n\t\t* const physicFormula = player.getFormulas('damagePhysic');\n\t\t* if (physicFormula) {\n\t\t*   const damage = physicFormula(attackerParams, defenderParams);\n\t\t* }\n\t\t* \n\t\t* // Get critical damage formula\n\t\t* const criticalFormula = player.getFormulas('damageCritical');\n\t\t* if (criticalFormula) {\n\t\t*   const criticalDamage = criticalFormula(baseDamage, attackerParams, defenderParams);\n\t\t* }\n\t\t* ```\n\t\t*/\n\t\tgetFormulas(name) {\n\t\t\treturn this.getCurrentMap()?.damageFormulas[name];\n\t\t}\n\t};\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/ClassManager.ts\n/**\n* Class Manager Mixin\n* \n* Provides class and actor management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n* character class assignment and actor setup, including automatic parameter configuration,\n* starting equipment, and skill progression based on class definitions.\n* \n* @param Base - The base class to extend with class management\n* @returns Extended class with class management methods\n* \n* @example\n* ```ts\n* class MyPlayer extends WithClassManager(BasePlayer) {\n*   constructor() {\n*     super();\n*     // Class system is automatically initialized\n*   }\n* }\n* \n* const player = new MyPlayer();\n* player.setClass(Fighter);\n* player.setActor(Hero);\n* ```\n*/\nfunction WithClassManager(Base) {\n\treturn class extends Base {\n\t\t_resolveClassInput(classInput, databaseByIdOverride) {\n\t\t\tif (isString(classInput)) return databaseByIdOverride ? databaseByIdOverride(classInput) : this.databaseById(classInput);\n\t\t\treturn classInput;\n\t\t}\n\t\t_createClassInstance(classInput) {\n\t\t\tconst classClass = this._resolveClassInput(classInput);\n\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\tclassClass,\n\t\t\t\tinstance: new classClass()\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Create a class instance without side effects.\n\t\t*/\n\t\tcreateClassInstance(classInput) {\n\t\t\treturn this._createClassInstance(classInput);\n\t\t}\n\t\t/**\n\t\t* Resolve class snapshot entry into a class instance without side effects.\n\t\t*/\n\t\tresolveClassSnapshot(snapshot, mapOverride) {\n\t\t\tif (!snapshot || snapshot._class == null) return snapshot;\n\t\t\tconst map = mapOverride ?? (this.getCurrentMap?.() || this.map);\n\t\t\tif (!map || !map.database) return snapshot;\n\t\t\tconst databaseByIdOverride = (id) => {\n\t\t\t\tconst data = map.database()[id];\n\t\t\t\tif (!data) throw new Error(`The ID=${id} data is not found in the database. Add the data in the property \"database\"`);\n\t\t\t\treturn data;\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tconst classId = isString(snapshot._class) ? snapshot._class : snapshot._class?.id;\n\t\t\tif (!classId) return snapshot;\n\t\t\tconst classClass = this._resolveClassInput(classId, databaseByIdOverride);\n\t\t\tconst { instance } = this._createClassInstance(classClass);\n\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\t...snapshot,\n\t\t\t\t_class: instance\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t}\n\t\tsetClass(_class) {\n\t\t\tconst { instance } = this._createClassInstance(_class);\n\t\t\tconst classInstance = instance;\n\t\t\tthis[\"execMethod\"](\"onSet\", [this], classInstance);\n\t\t\treturn classInstance;\n\t\t}\n\t\tsetActor(actorClass) {\n\t\t\tif (isString(actorClass)) actorClass = this.databaseById(actorClass);\n\t\t\tconst actor = new actorClass();\n\t\t\t[\n\t\t\t\t\"name\",\n\t\t\t\t\"initialLevel\",\n\t\t\t\t\"finalLevel\",\n\t\t\t\t\"expCurve\"\n\t\t\t].forEach((key) => {\n\t\t\t\tif (actor[key]) this[key] = actor[key];\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t\tfor (let param in actor.parameters) this.addParameter(param, actor.parameters[param]);\n\t\t\tfor (let item of actor.startingEquipment) {\n\t\t\t\tconst itemId = this.addItem(item)?.id?.();\n\t\t\t\tif (itemId) this.equip(itemId, true);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (actor.class) this.setClass(actor.class);\n\t\t\tthis[\"execMethod\"](\"onSet\", [this], actor);\n\t\t\treturn actor;\n\t\t}\n\t};\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/StateManager.ts\n/**\n* State Manager Mixin\n* \n* Provides state management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n* player states (buffs/debuffs), state defense from equipment, and state\n* efficiency modifiers. It manages the complete state system including\n* application, removal, and resistance mechanics.\n* \n* @param Base - The base class to extend with state management\n* @returns Extended class with state management methods\n* \n* @example\n* ```ts\n* class MyPlayer extends WithStateManager(BasePlayer) {\n*   constructor() {\n*     super();\n*     // State system is automatically initialized\n*   }\n* }\n* \n* const player = new MyPlayer();\n* player.addState(Paralyze);\n* console.log(player.getState(Paralyze));\n* ```\n*/\nfunction WithStateManager(Base) {\n\treturn class extends Base {\n\t\tconstructor(..._args) {\n\t\t\tsuper(..._args);\n\t\t\tthis._statesEfficiency = signal([]);\n\t\t}\n\t\t_getStateMap(required = true) {\n\t\t\tconst map = this.getCurrentMap?.() || this.map;\n\t\t\tif (required && (!map || !map.database)) throw new Error(\"Player must be on a map to resolve states\");\n\t\t\treturn map;\n\t\t}\n\t\t_resolveStateInput(stateInput, databaseByIdOverride) {\n\t\t\tif (isString(stateInput)) return databaseByIdOverride ? addStates, removeStates }) {\n\t\t\tif (addStates) for (let { state, rate } of addStates) player.addState(state, rate);\n\t\t\tif (removeStates) for (let { state, rate } of removeStates) player.removeState(state, rate);\n\t\t}\n\t\tgetState(stateClass) {\n\t\t\tif (isString(stateClass)) stateClass = this.databaseById(stateClass);\n\t\t\treturn this.states().find((state) => {\n\t\t\t\tif (isString(stateClass)) return state.id == stateClass;\n\t\t\t\treturn isInstanceOf(state, stateClass);\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t}\n\t\taddState(stateClass, chance = 1) {\n\t\t\tconst state = this.getState(stateClass);\n\t\t\tif (isString(stateClass)) stateClass = this.databaseById(stateClass);\n\t\t\tif (!state) {\n\t\t\t\tif (Math.random() > chance) throw StateLog.addFailed(stateClass);\n\t\t\t\tconst instance = this._createStateInstance(stateClass);\n\t\t\t\tthis.states().push(instance);\n\t\t\t\tthis.applyStates(this, instance);\n\t\t\t\treturn instance;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn null;\n\t\t}\n\t\tremoveState(stateClass, chance = 1) {\n\t\t\tconst index = this.states().findIndex((state) => {\n\t\t\t\tif (isString(stateClass)) return state.id == stateClass;\n\t\t\t\treturn isInstanceOf(state, stateClass);\n\t\t\t});\n\t\t\tif (index != -1) {\n\t\t\t\tif (Math.random() > chance) throw StateLog.removeFailed(stateClass);\n\t\t\t\tthis.states().splice(index, 1);\n\t\t\t} else throw StateLog.notApplied(stateClass);\n\t\t}\n\t\tfindStateEfficiency(stateClass) {\n\t\t\treturn this.statesEfficiency().find((state) => isInstanceOf(state.state, stateClass));\n\t\t}\n\t};\n}\n//#endregion\n//#region src/Player/Player.ts\n/**\n* Combines multiple RpgCommonPlayer mixins into resolvedSlot = resolveSaveSlot(slot, policy, this, context);\n\t\tif (resolvedSlot === null) return { ok: false };\n\t\tconst slotData = await resolveSaveStorageStrategy().get(this, resolvedSlot);\n\t\tif (!slotData?.snapshot) return { ok: false };\n\t\tawait this.applySnapshot(slotData.snapshot);\n\t\tconst { snapshot, ...meta } = slotData;\n\t\tif (options.changeMap !== false It is intended for custom client events\n\t* that are not already handled by built-in server actions such as\n\t* `move`, `action`, or GUI interactions.\n\t*\n\t* @title Listen to data from the client\n\t* @method player.on(key, cb)\n\t* @param key - Event name emitted by the client\n\t* @param cb - Callback invoked with the payload sent by the client\n\t* @returns {void}\n\t* @since 3.0.0-beta.5\n\t*\n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* player.on(\"chat:message\", ({ text }) => {\n\t*   console.log(\"Client says:\", text);\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*\n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* import { inject } from \"@rpgjs/client\";\n\t* import { WebSocketToken, type AbstractWebsocket } from \"@rpgjs/client\";\n\t*\n\t* const socket = inject<AbstractWebsocket>(WebSocketToken);\n\t* socket.emit(\"chat:message\", { text: \"Hello server\" });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\ton(key, cb) {\n\t\tthis._getClientListenerBucket(key).add(cb);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Listen one time to custom data sent by the current player's client.\n\t*\n\t* After the first matching event is received, the listener is removed\n\t* automatically.\n\t*\n\t* @title Listen one-time to data from the client\n\t* @method player.once(key, cb)\n\t* @param key - Event name emitted by the client\n\t* @param cb - Callback invoked only once with the payload sent by the client\n\t* @returns {void}\n\t* @since 3.0.0-beta.5\n\t*\n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* player.once(\"tutorial:ready\", (payload) => {\n\t*   console.log(\"Ready once:\", payload.step);\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tonce(key, cb) {\n\t\tconst onceCallback = async (data) => {\n\t\t\tthis._clientListeners.get(key)?.delete(onceCallback);\n\t\t\tawait cb(data);\n\t\t};\n\t\tthis.on(key, onceCallback);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Remove all listeners for a custom client event on this player.\n\t*\n\t* @title Remove listeners of the client event\n\t* @method player.off(key)\n\t* @param key - Normally, as soon as a hook is called in a class, the cycle is started. But you can start it manually\n\t* The method calls the `onChanges` method on events and synchronizes all map data with the client.\n\t\n\t* @title Run Sync Changes\n\t* @method player.syncChanges()\n\t* @returns {void}\n\t* @memberof Player\n\t*/\n\tsyncChanges() {\n\t\tthis._eventChanges();\n\t\tif (shouldAutoSave(this, {\n\t\t\treason: \"auto\",\n\t\t\tsource: \"syncChanges\"\n\t\t})) this.save(\"auto\", {}, {\n\t\t\treason: \"auto\",\n\t\t\tsource: \"syncChanges\"\n\t\t});\n\t}\n\tdatabaseById(id) {\n\t\tconst map = this.map;\n\t\tif (!map || !map.database) return;\n\t\tconst data = map.database()[id];\n\t\tif (!data) throw new Error(`The ID=${id} data is not found in the database. If not provided, a unique ID will be generated\n\t* @param options - Zone configuration options\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Create a circular detection zone\n\t* 120,\n\t*   direction: Direction.Right,\n\t*   limitedByWalls: true,\n\t* });\n\t* \n\t* // Create a zone with width/height (radius calculated automatically)\n\t* player.attachShape({\n\t*   width: is specified\");\n\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tshapeOptions = options;\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tzoneId = `zone-${this.id}-${Date.now()}`;\n\t\t\tshapeOptions = idOrOptions;\n\t\t}\n\t\tconst radius;\n\t\tif (shapeOptions.radius !== void 0) radius = shapeOptions.radius;\n\t\telse if (shapeOptions.width && shapeOptions.height) radius = Math.max(shapeOptions.width, shapeOptions.height) / 2;\n\t\telse {\n\t\t\tconsole.warn(\"attachShape: radius or width/height must be provided\");\n\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t}\n\t\tlet offset = new Vector2(0, 0);\n\t\tconst positioning = shapeOptions.positioning || \"default\";\n\t\tif (shapeOptions.positioning) {\n\t\t\tconst playerWidth = playerEntity.width || playerEntity.radius * 2 || 32;\n\t\t\tconst playerHeight = playerEntity.height || playerEntity.radius * 2 || 32;\n\t\t\tswitch (shapeOptions.positioning) {\n\t\t\t\tcase \"top\":\n\t\t\t\t\toffset = new Vector2(0, -playerHeight / 2);\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\tcase \"bottom\":\n\t\t\t\t\toffset = new Vector2(0, playerHeight / 2);\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\tcase \"left\":\n\t\t\t\t\toffset = new Vector2(-playerWidth / 2, 0);\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tconst zoneManager = map.physic.getZoneManager();\n\t\tlet direction = \"down\";\n\t\tif (shapeOptions.direction !== void 0) if (typeof shapeOptions.direction playerEntity.position.y + offset.y;\n\t\tconst physicZoneId = zoneManager.createAttachedZone(playerEntity, {\n\t\t\tradius,\n\t\t\tangle: shapeOptions.angle ?? 360,\n\t\t\tdirection,\n\t\t\tlimitedByWalls: shapeOptions.limitedByWalls ?? false,\n\t\t\toffset,\n\t\t\tmetadata: Object.keys(metadata).length > 0 ? metadata : void 0\n\t\t}, {\n\t\t\tonEnter: for this player only\n\t* \n\t* This method emits an event to play a sound only for this specific player.\n\t* The sound must be defined on the client side (in the client module configuration).\n\t* \n\t* ## Design\n\t* \n\t* The sound is sent only to this player's client connection, making it ideal\n\t* for personal feedback sounds like UI interactions, notifications, or personal\n\t* achievements. For map-wide sounds that all players should hear, use `map.playSound()` instead.\n\t* \n\t* @param soundId - Sound identifier, defined on the client side\n\t* @param options - Optional sound configuration\n\t* @param options.volume - The player or event that the camera should follow\n\t* @param options - Camera follow options\n\t* @param options.smoothMove - Enable smooth animation. Can be a boolean (default: true) or an object with animation parameters\n\t* @param options.smoothMove.time - Time duration for the animation in milliseconds (optional)\n\t* @param options.smoothMove.ease - Easing function name. Visit https://easings.net for available functions (optional)\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Follow another player with default smooth animation\n\t* player.cameraFollow(otherPlayer, { smoothMove: true });\n\t* \n\t* // Follow an event with custom smooth animation\n\t* player.cameraFollow(npcEvent, {\n\t*   smoothMove: {\n\t*     time: 1000,\n\t*     ease: \"easeInOutQuad\"\n\t*   }\n\t* });\n\t* \n\t* // Follow without animation (instant)\n\t* player.cameraFollow(targetPlayer, { smoothMove: false });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tcameraFollow(otherPlayer, options) {\n\t\tif (!this.getCurrentMap()) return;\n\t\tconst data = { targetId: otherPlayer.id };\n\t\tif (options?.smoothMove !== void 0) if (typeof options.smoothMove === \"boolean\") data.smoothMove = options.smoothMove;\n\t\telse data.smoothMove = {\n\t\t\tenabled: true,\n\t\t\t...options.smoothMove\n\t\t};\n\t\telse data.smoothMove = true;\n\t\tthis.emit(\"cameraFollow\", data);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Trigger a flash animation on this player\n\t* \n\t* This method sends a flash animation event to the client, creating a visual\n\t* feedback effect on the player's sprite. The flash can be configured with\n\t* various options including type (alpha, tint, or both), duration, cycles, and color.\n\t* \n\t* ## Design\n\t* \n\t* The flash is sent as a broadcast event to all clients viewing this player.\n\t* This is useful for visual feedback when the player takes damage, receives\n\t* a buff, or when an important event occurs.\n\t* \n\t* @param options - Flash configuration options\n\t* @param options.type - Type of flash effect: 'alpha' (opacity), 'tint' (color), or 'both' (default: 'alpha')\n\t* @param options.duration - Duration of the flash animation in milliseconds (default: 300)\n\t* @param options.cycles - Number of flash cycles (flash on/off) (default: 1)\n\t* @param options.alpha - Alpha value when flashing, from 0 to 1 (default: 0.3)\n\t* @param options.tint - Tint color when flashing as hex value or color name (default: 0xffffff - white)\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Simple flash with default settings (alpha flash)\n\t* player.flash();\n\t* \n\t* // Flash with red tint when taking damage\n\t* player.flash({ type: 'tint', tint: 0xff0000 });\n\t* \n\t* // Flash with both alpha and tint for dramatic effect\n\t* player.flash({ \n\t*   type: 'both', \n\t*   alpha: 0.5, \n\t*   tint: 0xff0000,\n\t*   duration: 200,\n\t*   cycles: 2\n\t* });\n\t* \n\t* // Quick damage flash\n\t* player.flash({ \n\t*   type: 'tint', \n\t*   tint: 'red', \n\t*   duration: 150,\n\t*   cycles: 1\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tflash(options) {\n\t\tconst map = this.getCurrentMap();\n\t\tif (!map) return;\n\t\tconst flashOptions = {\n\t\t\ttype: options?.type || \"alpha\",\n\t\t\tduration: options?.duration ?? `onOutShape` on players and events when they exit a shape\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* Uses the physics engine's collision event system to detect when entities collide.\n\t* When a collision is detected:\n\t* - Between a player and an event: triggers `onPlayerTouch` on the event\n\t* - Between a player/event and a shape: triggers `onInShape`/`onOutShape` hooks\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Event with onPlayerTouch hook\n\t* map.createDynamicEvent({\n\t*   x: 100,\n\t*   y: 200,\n\t*   event: {\n\t*     onPlayerTouch(player) {\n\t*       console.log(`Player ${player.id} console.log('in', player.name, shape.name);\n\t*   },\n\t*   onOutShape(player: RpgPlayer, shape: RpgShape) {\n\t*     console.log('out', player.name, shape.name);\n\t*   }\n\t* };\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tsetupCollisionDetection() {\n\t\tconst activeCollisions = /* @__PURE__ */ new Set();\n\t\tconst activeShapeCollisions = /* @__PURE__ */ new Set();\n\t\tconst hasDifferentZ = (entityA, entityB) => {\n\t\t\treturn entityA.owner?.z() !== entityB.owner?.z();\n\t\t};\n\t\tthis.physic.getEvents().onCollisionEnter((collision) => {\n\t\t\tconst entityA = collision.entityA;\n\t\t\tconst entityB = collision.entityB;\n\t\t\tif (hasDifferentZ(entityA, entityB)) return;\n\t\t\tconst collisionKey = entityA.uuid < entityB.uuid ? `${entityA.uuid}-${entityB.uuid}` : `${entityB.uuid}-${entityA.uuid}`;\n\t\t\tif (activeCollisions.has(collisionKey)) return;\n\t\t\tconst shapeA = this._shapeEntities.get(entityA.uuid);\n\t\t\tconst shapeB = this._shapeEntities.get(entityB.uuid);\n\t\t\tif (shapeA || shapeB) {\n\t\t\t\tconst shape = shapeA || shapeB;\n\t\t\t\tconst otherEntity = shapeA ? entityB : entityA;\n\t\t\t\tif (shape) this.getPlayer(entityB.uuid);\n\t\t\tif (!player) return;\n\t\t\tconst eventId = player.id === entityA.uuid ? entityB.uuid : entityA.uuid;\n\t\t\tconst event = this.getEvent(eventId);\n\t\t\tif (event && this.isEventVisibleForPlayer(eventId, player)) {\n\t\t\t\tactiveCollisions.add(collisionKey);\n\t\t\t\tevent.execMethod(\"onPlayerTouch\", [player]);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t});\n\t\tthis.physic.getEvents().onCollisionExit((collision) => {\n\t\t\tconst entityA = collision.entityA;\n\t\t\tconst entityB = collision.entityB;\n\t\t\tif (hasDifferentZ(entityA, entityB)) return;\n\t\t\tconst collisionKey = entityA.uuid < entityB.uuid ? `${entityA.uuid}-${entityB.uuid}` : `${entityB.uuid}-${entityA.uuid}`;\n\t\t\tconst shapeA = this._shapeEntities.get(entityA.uuid);\n\t\t\tconst shapeB = this._shapeEntities.get(entityB.uuid);\n\t\t\tif (shapeA || shapeB) {\n\t\t\t\tconst shape = shapeA || shapeB;\n\t\t\t\tconst otherEntity = shapeA ? entityB : entityA;\n\t\t\t\tif (shape) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst shapeKey = `${otherEntity.uuid}-${shape.name}`;\n\t\t\t\t\tif (activeShapeCollisions.has(shapeKey)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tactiveShapeCollisions.delete(shapeKey);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst player = this.getPlayer(otherEntity.uuid);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconst event = this.getEvent(otherEntity.uuid);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (player) player.execMethod(\"onOutShape\", [player, shape]);\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (event) event.execMethod(\"onOutShape\", [shape, player || event]);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tactiveCollisions.delete(collisionKey);\n\t\t});\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Intercepts and modifies packets before they are sent to clients\n\t* \n\t* This method is automatically called by @signe/room for each packet sent to clients.\n\t* It adds timestamp and acknowledgment information to sync packets for client-side\n\t* prediction reconciliation. This helps with network synchronization and reduces\n\t* perceived latency.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* Adds metadata to packets:\n\t* - `timestamp`: Current server time for client-side prediction\n\t* - `ack`: Acknowledgment info with last processed frame and authoritative position\n\t* \n\t* @param player - The player receiving the packet\n\t* @param packet - The packet data to intercept\n\t* @param conn - The connection object\n\t* @returns Modified packet with timestamp and ack info, or null if player is invalid\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // This method is called automatically by the framework\n\t* // You typically don't call it directly\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tinterceptorPacket(player, packet, conn) {\n\t\tlet obj = {};\n\t\tlet packetValue = packet?.value;\n\t\tif (!player) return null;\n\t\tif (packet && typeof packet === \"object\") {\n\t\t\tobj.timestamp = Date.now();\n\t\t\tif (player) {\n\t\t\t\tconst value = packet.value && typeof packet.value === \"object\" ? packet.value : void 0;\n\t\t\t\tconst playerSnapshot = (value?.players && typeof value.players === \"object\" ? value.players : void 0)?.[player.id];\n\t\t\t\tconst bodyPos = this.getBodyPosition(player.id, \"top-left\");\n\t\t\t\tconst ackX = typeof playerSnapshot?.x === \"number\" ? playerSnapshot.x : bodyPos?.x ?? player.x();\n\t\t\t\tconst ackY = typeof playerSnapshot?.y === \"number\" ? playerSnapshot.y : bodyPos?.y ?? player.y();\n\t\t\t\tobj.ack = {\n\t\t\t\t\tframe: player._lastFramePositions?.frame ?? 0,\n\t\t\t\t\tserverTick: this.getTick(),\n\t\t\t\t\tx: Math.round(ackX),\n\t\t\t\t\ty: Math.round(ackY),\n\t\t\t\t\tdirection: playerSnapshot?.direction ?? player.direction()\n\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (packetValue && typeof packetValue === \"object\" && packetValue.events && typeof packetValue.events === \"object\") {\n\t\t\tconst eventEntries = Object.entries(packetValue.events);\n\t\t\tconst filteredEntries = eventEntries.filter(([eventId]) => this.isEventVisibleForPlayer(eventId, player));\n\t\t\tif (filteredEntries.length !== eventEntries.length) {\n\t\t\t\tpacketValue = { ...packetValue };\n\t\t\t\tif (filteredEntries.length === 0) delete packetValue.events;\n\t\t\t\telse packetValue.events = Object.fromEntries(filteredEntries);\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (typeof packet.value == \"string\") return packet;\n\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t...packet,\n\t\t\tvalue: {\n\t\t\t\t...packetValue,\n\t\t\t\t...obj\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Called when a player joins the map\n\t* \n\t* This method is automatically called by @signe/room when a player connects to the map.\n\t* It initializes the player's connection, sets up the map context, and waits for\n\t* the map data to be ready before playing sounds and triggering hooks.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* 1. Sets player's map reference and context\n\t* 2. Initializes the player\n\t* 3. Waits for map data to be ready\n\t* 4. Plays map sounds for the player\n\t* 5. Triggers `server-player-onJoinMap` hook\n\t* \n\t* @param player - The player joining the map\n\t* @param conn - The connection object for the player\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // This method is called automatically by the framework\n\t* // You can listen to the hook to perform custom logic\n\t* server.addHook('server-player-onJoinMap', (player, map) => {\n\t*   console.log(`Player ${player.id} joined map ${map.id}`);\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tonJoin(player, conn) {\n\t\tconst alignPlayerBodyWithSignals = () => {\n\t\t\tconst hitbox = typeof player.hitbox === \"function\" ? player.hitbox() : player.hitbox;\n\t\t\tconst width = hitbox?.w ?? 32;\n\t\t\tconst height = hitbox?.h ?? 32;\n\t\t\tconst body = this.getBody(player.id);\n\t\t\tif (body) body.owner = player;\n\t\t\tthis.updateHitbox(player.id, player.x(), player.y(), width, height);\n\t\t};\n\t\tif (player.setMap) player.setMap(this);\n\t\telse player.map = this;\n\t\tplayer.context = context$1;\n\t\tplayer.conn = conn;\n\t\tplayer.pendingInputs = [];\n\t\tplayer.lastProcessedInputTs = 0;\n\t\tplayer._lastFramePositions = null;\n\t\tplayer._onInit();\n\t\talignPlayerBodyWithSignals();\n\t\tthis.dataIsReady$.pipe(finalize$1(() => {\n\t\t\t(async () => {\n\t\t\t\ttry {\n\t\t\t\t\talignPlayerBodyWithSignals();\n\t\t\t\t\tawait this.spawnScenarioEventsForPlayer(player);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (this.stopAllSoundsBeforeJoin) player.stopAllSounds();\n\t\t\t\t\tthis.sounds.forEach((sound) => player.playSound(sound, { loop: true }));\n\t\t\t\t\tplayer.emit(\"weatherState\", this.getWeather());\n\t\t\t\t\tawait lastValueFrom(this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-map-onJoin\", player, this));\n\t\t\t\t\tif (typeof this._onJoin === \"function\") await this._onJoin(player);\n\t\t\t\t\tawait lastValueFrom(this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-player-onJoinMap\", player, this));\n\t\t\t\t} catch (error) {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (isRpgLog(error)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tconsole.warn(`[RpgLog:${error.id}] ${error.message}`);\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tconsole.error(\"[RPGJS] Error during map onJoin hooks:\", error);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t})();\n\t\t})).subscribe();\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Called when a player leaves the map\n\t* \n\t* This method is automatically called by @signe/room when a player disconnects from the map.\n\t* It cleans up the player's pending inputs and triggers the appropriate hooks.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* 1. []];\n\t\tfor (const callback of listeners) await callback(player, data);\n\t}\n\tasync onSessionRestore(payload) {\n\t\treturn await BaseRoom.prototype.onSessionRestore.call(this, payload);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Handle GUI interaction from a player\n\t* \n\t* This method is called when a player interacts with a GUI element.\n\t* It synchronizes the player's changes to ensure the client state is up to date.\n\t* \n\t* @param player - The player performing the interaction\n\t* @param value - The interaction data from the client\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // This method is called automatically when a player interacts with a GUI\n\t* // The interaction data is sent from the client\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tasync guiInteraction(player, value) {\n\t\tconst gui = player.getGui(value.guiId);\n\t\tif (gui) await gui.emit(value.name, value.data);\n\t\tplayer.syncChanges();\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Handle GUI exit from a player\n\t* \n\t* This method is called when a player closes or exits a GUI.\n\t* It removes the GUI from the player's active GUIs.\n\t* \n\t* @param player - The player exiting the GUI\n\t* @param guiId - The ID of the GUI being exited\n\t* @param data - Optional data associated with the GUI exit\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // This method is called automatically when a player closes a GUI\n\t* // The GUI is removed from the player's active GUIs\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tguiExit(player, { guiId, data }) {\n\t\tplayer.removeGui(guiId, data);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Handle action input from a player\n\t* \n\t* This method is called when a player performs an action (like pressing a button).\n\t* It checks for collisions with events and triggers the appropriate hooks.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* 1. Gets all entities colliding with the player\n\t* 2. Triggers `onAction` hook on colliding events\n\t* 3. Triggers `onInput` hook on the player\n\t* \n\t* @param player - The player performing the action\n\t* @param action - The action data (button pressed, etc.)\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // This method is called automatically when a player presses an action button\n\t* // Events near the player will have their onAction hook triggered\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tonAction(player, action) {\n\t\tconst events = this.getCollisions(player.id).map((id) => this.getEvent(id)).filter((event) => !!event && this.isEventVisibleForPlayer(event, player));\n\t\tif (events.length > 0) events.forEach((event) => {\n\t\t\tevent.execMethod(\"onAction\", [player, action]);\n\t\t});\n\t\tplayer.execMethod(\"onInput\", [action]);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Handle movement input from a player\n\t* \n\t* This method is called when a player sends movement input from the client.\n\t* It queues the input for processing by the game loop. Inputs are processed\n\t* with frame numbers to ensure proper ordering and client-side prediction.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* - Inputs are queued in `player.pendingInputs`\n\t* - Duplicate frames are skipped to prevent processing the same input twice\n\t* - Inputs are processed asynchronously by the game loop\n\t* \n\t* @param player - The player sending the movement input\n\t* @param input - The input data containing frame number, input direction, and timestamp\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // This method is called automatically when a player moves\n\t* // The input is queued and processed by processInput()\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tasync onInput(player, input) {\n\t\tconst lastAckedFrame = player._lastFramePositions?.frame ?? 0;\n\t\tconst now = Date.now();\n\t\tconst candidates = [];\n\t\tconst enqueueCandidate = (entry) => {\n\t\t\tif (typeof entry?.frame !== \"number\") return;\n\t\t\tif (!entry?.input) return;\n\t\t\tconst candidate = {\n\t\t\t\tinput: entry.input,\n\t\t\t\tframe: entry.frame,\n\t\t\t\ttick: typeof entry.tick === \"number\" ? entry.tick : void 0,\n\t\t\t\ttimestamp: typeof entry.timestamp === \"number\" ? entry.timestamp : now\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tif (typeof entry.x === \"number\" && typeof entry.y === \"number\") candidate.clientState = {\n\t\t\t\tx: entry.x,\n\t\t\t\ty: entry.y,\n\t\t\t\tdirection: entry.direction\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t\tcandidates.push(candidate);\n\t\t};\n\t\tfor (const trajectoryEntry of Array.isArray(input?.trajectory) ? input.trajectory : []) enqueueCandidate(trajectoryEntry);\n\t\tenqueueCandidate(input);\n\t\tif (candidates.length === 0) return;\n\t\tcandidates.sort((a, b) => a.frame - b.frame);\n\t\tconst existingFrames = new Set(player.pendingInputs.map((pending) => pending?.frame).filter((frame) => typeof frame === \"number\"));\n\t\tfor (const candidate of candidates) {\n\t\t\tif (candidate.frame <= lastAckedFrame) continue;\n\t\t\tif (existingFrames.has(candidate.frame)) continue;\n\t\t\tplayer.pendingInputs.push(candidate);\n\t\t\texistingFrames.add(candidate.frame);\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tonPing(player, payload) {\n\t\tplayer.emit(\"pong\", {\n\t\t\tserverTick: this.getTick(),\n\t\t\tclientTime: typeof payload?.clientTime === \"number\" ? payload.clientTime : Date.now(),\n\t\t\tclientFrame: typeof payload?.clientFrame === \"number\" ? payload.clientFrame : 0\n\t\t});\n\t}\n\tasync saveSlot(player, value) {\n\t\tBaseRoom.prototype.saveSlot(player, value);\n\t}\n\tasync loadSlot(player, value) {\n\t\tBaseRoom.prototype.loadSlot(player, value);\n\t}\n\tasync listSaveSlots(player, value) {\n\t\treturn await BaseRoom.prototype.listSaveSlots(player, value);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Listen to custom websocket events sent by clients on this map.\n\t*\n\t* The callback receives the player who sent the event and the payload.\n\t* This is useful for map-wide custom interactions that are not covered\n\t* by built-in actions such as movement, GUI events, or the action button.\n\t*\n\t* @method map.on(type, cb)\n\t* @param type - Custom event name emitted by clients\n\t* @param cb - Callback invoked with the sending player and payload\n\t* @returns {void}\n\t*\n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* map.on(\"chat:message\", (player, data) => {\n\t*   console.log(player.id, data.text);\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\ton(type, cb) {\n\t\tthis._getClientListenerBucket(type).add(cb);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Remove all listeners for a custom client event on this map.\n\t*\n\t* @method map.off(type)\n\t* @param type - Custom event name to clear\n\t* @returns {void}\n\t*/\n\toff(type) {\n\t\tthis._clientListeners.delete(type);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Broadcast a custom websocket event to all clients connected to this map.\n\t*\n\t* This is a convenience wrapper around `$broadcast({ type, value })`.\n\t* On the client side, receive the event by injecting `WebSocketToken`\n\t* and subscribing with `socket.on(type, cb)`.\n\t*\n\t* @method map.broadcast(type, value)\n\t* @param type - Custom event name sent to all clients on the map\n\t* @param value - Payload sent with the event\n\t* @returns {void}\n\t*\n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* map.broadcast(\"weather:warning\", {\n\t*   level: \"storm\",\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*\n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* import { inject } from \"@rpgjs/client\";\n\t* import { WebSocketToken, type AbstractWebsocket } from \"@rpgjs/client\";\n\t*\n\t* const socket = inject<AbstractWebsocket>(WebSocketToken);\n\t*\n\t* socket.on(\"weather:warning\", (payload) => {\n\t*   console.log(payload.level);\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tbroadcast(type, value) {\n\t\tthis.$broadcast({\n\t\t\ttype,\n\t\t\tvalue\n\t\t});\n\t}\n\tasync _onUnhandledAction(player, message) {\n\t\tif (!player) return;\n\t\tawait player._dispatchClientEvent(message.action, message.value);\n\t\tawait this._dispatchClientEvent(message.action, player, message.value);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Update the map configuration and data\n\t* \n\t* This endpoint receives map data from the client and initializes the map.\n\t* It loads the map configuration, damage formulas, events, and physics.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* 1. [];\n\t\tlet initialWeather = this.globalConfig?.weather;\n\t\tif (map.id) {\n\t\t\tconst mapFound = this.maps.find((m) => m.id === map.id);\n\t\t\tif (typeof mapFound?.weather !== \"undefined\") initialWeather = mapFound.weather;\n\t\t\tif (mapFound?.events) map.events = [...mapFound.events, ...map.events];\n\t\t\tif (mapFound?.sounds) this.sounds = [...map.sounds ?? [];\n\t\t\tif (mapFound?.onLoad) this._onLoad = mapFound.onLoad;\n\t\t\tif (mapFound?.onJoin) this._onJoin = mapFound.onJoin;\n\t\t\tif (mapFound?.onLeave) this._onLeave = mapFound.onLeave;\n\t\t\tif (mapFound?.stopAllSoundsBeforeJoin !== void 0) this.stopAllSoundsBeforeJoin = mapFound.stopAllSoundsBeforeJoin;\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (typeof initialWeather !== \"undefined\") this.setWeather(initialWeather);\n\t\telse this.clearWeather();\n\t\tawait lastValueFrom(this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-map-onBeforeUpdate\", map, this));\n\t\tthis._scenarioEventTemplates = [];\n\t\tthis._eventModeById.clear();\n\t\tthis._eventOwnerById.clear();\n\t\tthis._scenarioEventIdsByPlayer.clear();\n\t\tthis.loadPhysic();\n\t\tfor (let event of map.events ?? The world ID is extracted from the URL path.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* 1. Extracts world ID from URL path parameter\n\t* 2. Normalizes input to array of WorldMapConfig\n\t* 3. Ensures all required map properties are present (width, height, tile sizes)\n\t* 4. Creates or updates the world manager\n\t* \n\t* Expected payload examples:\n\t* - `{ id: string, maps: WorldMapConfig[] }`\n\t* - `WorldMapConfig[]`\n\t* \n\t* @param request - HTTP request containing world configuration\n\t* @returns Promise resolving to `{ ok: true }` when complete\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // POST /world/my-world/update\n\t* // Body: [{ id: 'map1', worldX: 0, worldY: 0, width: 800, height: 600 }]\n\t* \n\t* // Or with nested structure\n\t* // Body: { id: 'my-world', maps: [{ id: 'map1', ... }] }\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tasync updateWorld(request) {\n\t\tlet worldId = \"\";\n\t\ttry {\n\t\t\tconst reqUrl = request.url;\n\t\t\tworldId = new URL(reqUrl, \"http://localhost\").pathname.split(\"/\")[2] ?? \"\";\n\t\t} catch {}\n\t\tconst payload = await request.json();\n\t\tconst normalized = (Array.isArray(payload) ? payload : payload?.maps ?? []).map((m) => {\n\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\tid: m.id,\n\t\t\t\tworldX: m.worldX ?? m.x ?? 0,\n\t\t\t\tworldY: m.worldY ?? m.y ?? 0,\n\t\t\t\twidth: m.width ?? m.widthPx ?? this.data()?.width ?? 0,\n\t\t\t\theight: m.height ?? m.heightPx ?? this.data()?.height ?? 0,\n\t\t\t\ttileWidth: m.tileWidth ?? this.tileWidth ?? 32,\n\t\t\t\ttileHeight: m.tileHeight ?? this.tileHeight ?? 32\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t});\n\t\tawait this.updateWorldMaps(worldId, normalized);\n\t\treturn { ok: true };\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Process pending inputs for a player with anti-cheat validation\n\t* \n\t* This method processes pending inputs for a player while performing\n\t* anti-cheat validation to prevent time manipulation and frame skipping.\n\t* It validates the time deltas between inputs and ensures they are within\n\t* acceptable ranges. To preserve movement itinerary under network bursts,\n\t* the number of inputs processed per call is capped.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* **Important**: This method only updates entity velocities - it does NOT step\n\t* the physics engine. Physics simulation is handled centrally by the game loop\n\t* (`tick$` -> `runFixedTicks`). This ensures:\n\t* - Consistent physics timing (60fps fixed timestep)\n\t* - No double-stepping when multiple inputs are processed\n\t* - Deterministic physics regardless of input frequency\n\t* \n\t* @param playerId - The ID of the player to process inputs for\n\t* @param controls - Optional anti-cheat configuration\n\t* @returns Promise containing the player and processed input strings\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Process inputs with default anti-cheat settings\n\t* const result = await map.processInput('player1');\n\t* console.log('Processed inputs:', result.inputs);\n\t* \n\t* // Process inputs with custom anti-cheat configuration\n\t* const result = await map.processInput('player1', {\n\t*   maxTimeDelta: 100,\n\t*   maxFrameDelta: 5,\n\t*   minTimeBetweenInputs: 16,\n\t*   enableAntiCheat: true\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tasync processInput(playerId, controls) {\n\t\tconst player = this.getPlayer(playerId);\n\t\tif (!player) throw new Error(`Player ${playerId} not found`);\n\t\tif (!player.isConnected()) {\n\t\t\tplayer.pendingInputs = [];\n\t\t\treturn {\n\t\t\t\tplayer,\n\t\t\t\tinputs: []\n\t\t\t};\n\t\t}\n\t\tconst processedInputs = [];\n\t\tconst config = {\n\t\t\tmaxTimeDelta: 1e3,\n\t\t\tmaxFrameDelta: 10,\n\t\t\tminTimeBetweenInputs: 16,\n\t\t\tenableAntiCheat: false,\n\t\t\tmaxInputsPerTick: 1,\n\t\t\t...controls\n\t\t};\n\t\tlet lastProcessedTime = player.lastProcessedInputTs || 0;\n\t\tlet lastProcessedFrame = player._lastFramePositions?.frame ?? 0;\n\t\tplayer.pendingInputs.sort((a, b) => (a.frame || 0) - (b.frame || 0));\n\t\tlet hasProcessedInputs = false;\n\t\tlet processedThisTick = 0;\n\t\twhile (player.pendingInputs.length > 0 && processedThisTick < config.maxInputsPerTick) {\n\t\t\tconst input = player.pendingInputs.shift();\n\t\t\tif (!input || typeof input.frame !== \"number\") continue;\n\t\t\tif (config.enableAntiCheat) {\n\t\t\t\tif (input.frame > lastProcessedFrame + config.maxFrameDelta) input.frame = lastProcessedFrame + 1;\n\t\t\t\tif (input.timestamp && lastProcessedTime > 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (input.timestamp - lastProcessedTime > config.maxTimeDelta) input.timestamp = lastProcessedTime + config.minTimeBetweenInputs;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif (input.timestamp && lastProcessedTime > 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (input.timestamp - lastProcessedTime < config.minTimeBetweenInputs) continue;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (input.frame < lastProcessedFrame - 10) continue;\n\t\t\tif (input.input) {\n\t\t\t\tawait this.movePlayer(player, input.input);\n\t\t\t\tprocessedInputs.push(input.input);\n\t\t\t\thasProcessedInputs = true;\n\t\t\t\tlastProcessedTime = input.timestamp || Date.now();\n\t\t\t\tprocessedThisTick += 1;\n\t\t\t\tconst bodyPos = this.getBodyPosition(player.id, \"top-left\");\n\t\t\t\tconst ackX = typeof input.clientState?.x === \"number\" ? input.clientState.x : bodyPos?.x ?? player.x();\n\t\t\t\tconst ackY = typeof input.clientState?.y === \"number\" ? input.clientState.y : bodyPos?.y ?? player.y();\n\t\t\t\tplayer._lastFramePositions = {\n\t\t\t\t\tframe: input.frame,\n\t\t\t\t\tposition: {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tx: Math.round(ackX),\n\t\t\t\t\t\ty: Math.round(ackY),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdirection: input.clientState?.direction ?? player.direction()\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\tserverTick: this.getTick()\n\t\t\t\t};\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlastProcessedFrame = input.frame;\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (hasProcessedInputs) player.lastProcessedInputTs = lastProcessedTime;\n\t\telse {\n\t\t\tconst idleTimeout = Math.max(config.minTimeBetweenInputs * 4, 50);\n\t\t\tconst lastTs = player.lastProcessedInputTs || 0;\n\t\t\tif (lastTs > 0 && Date.now() - lastTs > idleTimeout) {\n\t\t\t\tthis.stopMovement(player);\n\t\t\t\tplayer.lastProcessedInputTs = 0;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn {\n\t\t\tplayer,\n\t\t\tinputs: processedInputs\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Main game loop that processes player inputs\n\t* \n\t* This private method subscribes to tick$ and processes pending inputs\n\t* for all players on the map with a throttle of 50ms. It ensures inputs are\n\t* processed in order and prevents concurrent processing for the same player.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* - Subscribes to tick$ with throttleTime(50ms) for responsive input processing\n\t* - Processes inputs for each player with pending inputs\n\t* - Uses a flag to prevent concurrent processing for the same player\n\t* - Calls `processInput()` to handle anti-cheat validation and movement\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // This method is called automatically in the constructor if autoTick is enabled\n\t* // You typically don't call it directly\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tloop() {\n\t\tif (this._inputLoopSubscription) this._inputLoopSubscription.unsubscribe();\n\t\tthis._inputLoopSubscription = this.tick$.subscribe(() => {\n\t\t\tfor (const player of this.getPlayers()) {\n\t\t\t\tconst anyPlayer = player;\n\t\t\t\tif (!(player.pendingInputs.length > 0 || (player.lastProcessedInputTs || 0) > 0) || anyPlayer._isProcessingInputs) continue;\n\t\t\t\tanyPlayer._isProcessingInputs = true;\n\t\t\t\tthis.processInput(player.id).finally(() => {\n\t\t\t\t\tanyPlayer._isProcessingInputs = false;\n\t\t\t\t});\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t});\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Enable or disable automatic tick processing\n\t* \n\t* When disabled, the input processing loop will not run automatically.\n\t* This is useful for unit tests where you want manual control over when\n\t* inputs are processed.\n\t* \n\t* @param enabled - Whether to enable automatic tick processing (default: true)\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Disable auto tick for testing\n\t* map.setAutoTick(false);\n\t* \n\t* // Manually trigger tick processing\n\t* await map.processInput('player1');\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tsetAutoTick(enabled) {\n\t\tthis._autoTickEnabled = enabled;\n\t\tif (enabled && !this._inputLoopSubscription) this.loop();\n\t\telse if (!enabled && this._inputLoopSubscription) {\n\t\t\tthis._inputLoopSubscription.unsubscribe();\n\t\t\tthis._inputLoopSubscription = void 0;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get a world manager by id\n\t* \n\t* Returns the world maps manager for the given world ID. Currently, only\n\t* one world manager is supported per map instance.\n\t* \n\t* @param id - The world ID (currently unused, returns the single manager)\n\t* @returns The WorldMapsManager instance, or null if not initialized\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const worldManager = map.getWorldMaps('my-world');\n\t* if (worldManager) {\n\t*   const mapInfo = worldManager.getMapInfo('map1');\n\t* }\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetWorldMaps(id) {\n\t\tif (!this.worldMapsManager) return null;\n\t\treturn this.worldMapsManager;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Delete a world manager by id\n\t* \n\t* Removes the world maps manager from this map instance. Currently, only\n\t* one world manager is supported, so this clears the single manager.\n\t* \n\t* @param id - The world ID (currently unused)\n\t* @returns true if the manager was deleted, false if it didn't exist\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const deleted = map.deleteWorldMaps('my-world');\n\t* if (deleted) {\n\t*   console.log('World manager removed');\n\t* }\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tdeleteWorldMaps(id) {\n\t\tif (!this.worldMapsManager) return false;\n\t\tthis.worldMapsManager = void 0;\n\t\treturn true;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Create a world manager dynamically\n\t* \n\t* Creates a new WorldMapsManager instance and configures it with the provided\n\t* map configurations. This is used when loading world data from Tiled or\n\t* other map editors.\n\t* \n\t* @param world - World configuration object\n\t* @param world.id - Optional world identifier\n\t* @param world.maps - Array of map configurations for the world\n\t* @returns The newly created WorldMapsManager instance\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const manager = map.createDynamicWorldMaps({\n\t*   id: 'my-world',\n\t*   maps: [\n\t*     { id: 'map1', worldX: 0, worldY: 0, width: 800, height: 600 },\n\t*     { id: 'map2', worldX: 800, worldY: 0, width: 800, height: 600 }\n\t*   ]\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tcreateDynamicWorldMaps(world) {\n\t\tconst manager = new WorldMapsManager();\n\t\tmanager.configure(world.maps);\n\t\tthis.worldMapsManager = manager;\n\t\treturn manager;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Update world maps by id. Auto-create when missing.\n\t* \n\t* Updates the world maps configuration. If the world manager doesn't exist,\n\t* it is automatically created. This is useful for dynamically loading world\n\t* data or updating map positions.\n\t* \n\t* @param id - The world ID\n\t* @param maps - Array of map configurations to update\n\t* @returns Promise that resolves when the update is complete\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* await map.updateWorldMaps('my-world', [\n\t*   { id: 'map1', worldX: 0, worldY: 0, width: 800, height: 600 },\n\t*   { id: 'map2', worldX: 800, worldY: 0, width: 800, height: 600 }\n\t* ]);\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tasync updateWorldMaps(id, maps) {\n\t\tlet world = this.getWorldMaps(id);\n\t\tif (!world) world = this.createDynamicWorldMaps({\n\t\t\tid,\n\t\t\tmaps\n\t\t});\n\t\telse world.configure(maps);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Add data to the map's database\n\t* \n\t* This method delegates to BaseRoom's implementation to avoid code duplication.\n\t* \n\t* @param id - Unique identifier for the data\n\t* @param data - The data to store (can be a class, object, or any value)\n\t* @param options - Optional configuration\n\t* @param options.force - If true, overwrites existing data even if ID already exists (default: false)\n\t* @returns true if data was added, false if ignored (ID already exists)\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Add an item class to the database\n\t* map.addInDatabase('Potion', PotionClass);\n\t* \n\t* // Add an item object to the database\n\t* map.addInDatabase('custom-item', {\n\t*   name: 'Custom Item',\n\t*   price: 100\n\t* });\n\t* \n\t* // Force overwrite existing data\n\t* map.addInDatabase('Potion', UpdatedPotionClass, { force: true });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\taddInDatabase(id, data, options) {\n\t\treturn BaseRoom.prototype.addInDatabase.call(this, id, data, options);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Remove data from the map's database\n\t* \n\t* This method delegates to BaseRoom's implementation to avoid code duplication.\n\t* \n\t* @param id - Unique identifier of the data to remove\n\t* @returns true if data was removed, false if ID didn't exist\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Remove an item from the database\n\t* map.removeInDatabase('Potion');\n\t* \n\t* // Check if removal was successful\n\t* const removed = map.removeInDatabase('custom-item');\n\t* if (removed) {\n\t*   console.log('Item removed successfully');\n\t* }\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tremoveInDatabase(id) {\n\t\treturn BaseRoom.prototype.removeInDatabase.call(this, id);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Creates a dynamic event on the map\n\t* \n\t* This method handles both class-based events and object-based events with hooks.\n\t* For class-based events, it creates a new instance of the class.\n\t* For object-based events, it creates a dynamic class that extends RpgEvent and \n\t* implements the hook methods from the object.\n\t* \n\t* @param eventObj - The event position and definition\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* // Using a class-based event\n\t* class MyEvent extends RpgEvent {\n\t*   onInit() {\n\t*     console.log('Event initialized');\n\t*   }\n\t* }\n\t* \n\t* map.createDynamicEvent({\n\t*   x: 100,\n\t*   y: 200,\n\t*   event: MyEvent\n\t* });\n\t* \n\t* // Using an object-based event\n\t* map.createDynamicEvent({\n\t*   x: 100,\n\t*   y: 200,\n\t*   event: {\n\t*     onInit() {\n\t*       console.log('Event initialized');\n\t*     },\n\t*     onPlayerTouch(player) {\n\t*       console.log('Player touched event');\n\t*     }\n\t*   }\n\t* });\n\t*/\n\tasync createDynamicEvent(eventObj, options = {}) {\n\t\teventObj = this.normalizeEventObject(eventObj);\n\t\t(await lastValueFrom(this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-event-onBeforeCreated\", eventObj, this))).filter((v) => v).forEach((v) => {\n\t\t\teventObj = v;\n\t\t});\n\t\tconst event = eventObj.event;\n\t\tconst x = typeof eventObj.x === \"number\" ? eventObj.x : 0;\n\t\tconst y = typeof eventObj.y === \"number\" ? eventObj.y : 0;\n\t\tconst requestedMode = options.mode ?? this.resolveEventMode(eventObj);\n\t\tconst mode = this.normalizeEventMode(requestedMode);\n\t\tconst ownerFromData = options.scenarioOwnerId ?? this.resolveScenarioOwnerId(eventObj);\n\t\tconst scenarioOwnerId = mode === EventMode.Scenario ? ownerFromData : void 0;\n\t\tconst effectiveMode = mode === EventMode.Scenario && scenarioOwnerId ? EventMode.Scenario : EventMode.Shared;\n\t\tif (mode === EventMode.Scenario && !scenarioOwnerId) console.warn(\"Scenario event created without owner id. Falling back to shared mode.\");\n\t\tconst id = this.buildRuntimeEventId(eventObj.id, effectiveMode, scenarioOwnerId);\n\t\tlet eventInstance;\n\t\tif (this.events()[id]) {\n\t\t\tconsole.warn(`Event ${id} already exists on map`);\n\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (typeof event === \"function\") {\n\t\t\teventInstance = new event();\n\t\t\tif (event.prototype.name) eventInstance.name.set(event.prototype.name);\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tclass DynamicEvent extends RpgEvent {\n\t\t\t\tconstructor() {\n\t\t\t\t\tsuper();\n\t\t\t\t\tconst hookObj = event;\n\t\t\t\t\tif (hookObj.onInit) this.onInit = hookObj.onInit.bind(this);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (hookObj.onChanges) this.onChanges = hookObj.onChanges.bind(this);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (hookObj.onAction) this.onAction = hookObj.onAction.bind(this);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (hookObj.onPlayerTouch) this.onPlayerTouch = hookObj.onPlayerTouch.bind(this);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (hookObj.onInShape) this.onInShape = hookObj.onInShape.bind(this);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (hookObj.onOutShape) this.onOutShape = hookObj.onOutShape.bind(this);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (hookObj.onDetectInShape) this.onDetectInShape = hookObj.onDetectInShape.bind(this);\n\t\t\t\t\tif (hookObj.onDetectOutShape) this.onDetectOutShape = hookObj.onDetectOutShape.bind(this);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\teventInstance = new DynamicEvent();\n\t\t\tif (event.name) eventInstance.name.set(event.name);\n\t\t}\n\t\teventInstance.id = id;\n\t\teventInstance.mode = effectiveMode;\n\t\tif (effectiveMode === EventMode.Scenario && scenarioOwnerId) {\n\t\t\teventInstance._scenarioOwnerId = scenarioOwnerId;\n\t\t\teventInstance.scenarioOwnerId = scenarioOwnerId;\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdelete eventInstance._scenarioOwnerId;\n\t\t\tdelete eventInstance.scenarioOwnerId;\n\t\t}\n\t\teventInstance.map = this;\n\t\teventInstance.context = context$1;\n\t\tawait eventInstance.teleport({\n\t\t\tx,\n\t\t\ty\n\t\t});\n\t\tawait eventInstance.execMethod(\"onInit\");\n\t\tthis.events()[id] = eventInstance;\n\t\tthis.setEventRuntimeMetadata(id, effectiveMode, scenarioOwnerId);\n\t\treturn id;\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get an event by its ID\n\t* \n\t* Returns the event with the specified ID, or undefined if not found.\n\t* The return type can be narrowed using TypeScript generics.\n\t* \n\t* @param eventId - The unique identifier of the event\n\t* @returns The event instance, or undefined if not found\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Get any event\n\t* const event = map.getEvent('npc-1');\n\t* \n\t* // Get event with type narrowing\n\t* const npc = map.getEvent<MyNPC>('npc-1');\n\t* if (npc) {\n\t*   npc.speak('Hello!');\n\t* }\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetEvent(eventId) {\n\t\treturn this.events()[eventId];\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get a player by their ID\n\t* \n\t* Returns the player with the specified ID, or undefined if not found.\n\t* \n\t* @param playerId - The unique identifier of the player\n\t* @returns The player instance, or undefined if not found\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const player = map.getPlayer('player-123');\n\t* if (player) {\n\t*   console.log(`Player ${player.name} is on the map`);\n\t* }\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetPlayer(playerId) {\n\t\treturn this.players()[playerId];\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get all players currently on the map\n\t* \n\t* Returns an array of all players that are currently connected to this map.\n\t* \n\t* @returns Array of all RpgPlayer instances on the map\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const players = map.getPlayers();\n\t* console.log(`There are ${players.length} players on the map`);\n\t* \n\t* players.forEach(player => {\n\t*   console.log(`- ${player.name}`);\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetPlayers() {\n\t\treturn Object.values(this.players());\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get all events on the map\n\t* \n\t* Returns an array of all events (NPCs, objects, etc.) that are currently\n\t* on this map.\n\t* \n\t* @returns Array of all RpgEvent instances on the map\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* const events = map.getEvents();\n\t* console.log(`There are ${events.length} events on the map`);\n\t* \n\t* events.forEach(event => {\n\t*   console.log(`- ${event.name} at (${event.x}, ${event.y})`);\n\t* });\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetEvents() {\n\t\treturn Object.values(this.events());\n\t}\n\tgetEventsForPlayer(playerOrId) {\n\t\treturn this.getEvents().filter((event) => this.isEventVisibleForPlayer(event, playerOrId));\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get the first event that matches a condition\n\t* \n\t* Searches through all events on the map and returns the first one that\n\t* matches the provided callback function.\n\t* \n\t* @param cb - Callback function that returns true for the desired event\n\t* @returns The first matching event, or undefined if none found\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Find an event by name\n\t* const npc = map.getEventBy(event => event.name === 'Merchant');\n\t* \n\t* // Find an event at a specific position\n\t* const chest = map.getEventBy(event => \n\t*   event.x === 100 && event.y === 200\n\t* );\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetEventBy(cb) {\n\t\treturn this.getEventsBy(cb)[0];\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get all events that match a condition\n\t* \n\t* Searches through all events on the map and returns all events that\n\t* match the provided callback function.\n\t* \n\t* @param cb - Callback function that returns true for desired events\n\t* @returns Array of all matching events\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Find all NPCs\n\t* const npcs = map.getEventsBy(event => event.name.startsWith('NPC-'));\n\t* \n\t* // Find all events in a specific area\n\t* const nearbyEvents = map.getEventsBy(event => \n\t*   event.x >= 0 && event.x <= 100 &&\n\t*   event.y >= 0 && event.y <= 100\n\t* );\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tgetEventsBy(cb) {\n\t\treturn this.getEvents().filter(cb);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Remove an event from the map\n\t* \n\t* Removes the event with the specified ID from the map. { ...weather.params } : void 0\n\t\t};\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Get the current map weather state.\n\t*/\n\tgetWeather() {\n\t\treturn this.cloneWeatherState(this._weatherState);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Set the full weather state for this map.\n\t*\n\t* When `sync` is true (default), all connected clients receive the new weather.\n\t*/\n\tsetWeather(next, options = {}) {\n\t\tconst sync = options.sync !== false;\n\t\tif (next && !next.effect) throw new Error(\"setWeather: 'effect' is required when weather is not null.\");\n\t\tthis._weatherState = this.cloneWeatherState(next);\n\t\tif (sync) this.$broadcast({\n\t\t\ttype: \"weatherState\",\n\t\t\tvalue: this._weatherState\n\t\t});\n\t\treturn this.getWeather();\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Patch the current weather state.\n\t*\n\t* Nested `params` values are merged.\n\t*/\n\tpatchWeather(patch, options = {}) {\n\t\tconst current = this._weatherState ?? null;\n\t\tif (!current && !patch.effect) throw new Error(\"patchWeather: 'effect' is required when no weather is currently set.\");\n\t\tconst next = {\n\t\t\t...current ?? {},\n\t\t\t...patch,\n\t\t\tparams: {\n\t\t\t\t...current?.params ?? {},\n\t\t\t\t...patch.params ?? {}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t};\n\t\treturn this.setWeather(next, options);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Clear weather for this map.\n\t*/\n\tclearWeather(options = {}) {\n\t\tthis.setWeather(null, options);\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Configure runtime synchronized properties on the map\n\t* \n\t* This method allows you to dynamically add synchronized properties to the map\n\t* that will be automatically synced with clients. The schema follows the same\n\t* structure as module properties with `$initial`, `$syncWithClient`, and `$permanent` options.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* - Reads a schema object shaped like module props\n\t* - Creates typed sync signals with @signe/sync\n\t* - Properties are accessible as `map.propertyName`\n\t* \n\t* @param schema - Schema object defining the properties to sync\n\t* @param schema[key].$initial - Initial value for the property\n\t* @param schema[key].$syncWithClient - Whether to sync this property to clients\n\t* @param schema[key].$permanent - Whether to persist this property\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* // Add synchronized properties to the map\n\t* map.setSync({\n\t*   weather: {\n\t*     $initial: 'sunny',\n\t*     $syncWithClient: true,\n\t*     $permanent: false\n\t*   },\n\t*   timeOfDay: {\n\t*     $initial: 12,\n\t*     $syncWithClient: true,\n\t*     $permanent: false\n\t*   }\n\t* });\n\t* \n\t* // Use the properties\n\t* map.weather.set('rainy');\n\t* const currentWeather = map.weather();\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tsetSync(schema) {\n\t\tfor (let key in schema) this[key] = type(signal(typeof schema[key]?.$initial !== \"undefined\" ? schema[key].$initial : null), key, {\n\t\t\tsyncToClient: schema[key]?.$syncWithClient,\n\t\t\tpersist: schema[key]?.$permanent\n\t\t}, {});\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Apply sync to the client\n\t* \n\t* This method applies sync to the client by calling the `$applySync()` method.\n\t* \n\t* @example\n\t* ```ts\n\t* map.applySyncToClient();\n\t* ```\n\t*/\n\tapplySyncToClient() {\n\t\tthis.$applySync();\n\t}\n\t/**\n\t* Create a shape dynamically on the map\n\t* \n\t* This method creates a static hitbox on the map that can be used for\n\t* collision detection, area triggers, or visual boundaries. The shape is\n\t* backed by the physics engine's static entity system for accurate collision detection.\n\t* \n\t* ## Architecture\n\t* \n\t* Creates a static entity (hitbox) in the physics engine at the specified position and size.\n\t* The shape is stored internally and can be retrieved by name. When players or events\n\t* collide with this hitbox, the `onInShape` and `onOutShape` hooks are automatically\n\t* triggered on both the player and the event.\n\t* \n\t* @param obj - Shape Z position/depth for rendering (optional)\n\t* @param obj.color - Color in hexadecimal format, shared with client (optional)\n\t* @param obj.collision - Whether the shape has collision (optional)\n\t* (obj.collision !== void 0) properties.collision = obj.collision;\n\t\tconst shape = new RpgShape({\n\t\t\tname,\n\t\t\tpositioning: \"default\",\n\t\t\twidth,\n\t\t\theight,\n\t\t\tx:
